```text
     1→# UI Gap Implementation Plan
     2→
     3→**Created:** 2026-03-19  
     4→**Status:** Phases 1–5 complete (2026-03-21); Phase 6 (UX/Accessibility) complete (2026-03-20); Refactor backlog M01–M27 complete (2026-03-21)  
     5→**Source basis:** `tasks/ui-review.md` and `tasks/gaps.md` refreshed on 2026-03-19
     6→
     7→## Overview
     8→
     9→This plan translates the March 19 source-verified UI review into an implementation sequence that closes the highest-risk workflow gaps first, then finishes story-completeness across ATS, intake, rerun, review ergonomics, and accessibility.
    10→
    11→The plan is intentionally staged. The current app has meaningful workflow infrastructure already, so the fastest path is not a full redesign. We should preserve the existing review pipeline and add the missing workflow contracts, UI surfaces, persistence, and validation layers in slices that remain testable throughout.
    12→
    13→## Current State
    14→
    15→- Rewrite review, publication review, workflow state basics, and parts of finalise, harvest, and rerun are real.
    16→- The biggest missing behavior is the staged generation contract: `HTML preview -> layout review -> confirmed final generation`.
    17→- ATS support exists, but scoring, document semantics, and visibility are still below the reviewed story target.
    18→- Intake, rerun, and spell-check flows are partially implemented but not dependable enough to support iterative use.
    19→- UX, accessibility, and responsive behavior need a focused cleanup pass after the core workflow is complete.
    20→
    21→## Scope
    22→
    23→This plan covers the open or partial gaps that most directly affect end-to-end success:
    24→
    25→- `GAP-20` staged generation workflow
    26→- `GAP-21` ATS match score and keyword visibility
    27→- `GAP-22` ATS document structure and skill semantics
    28→- `GAP-23` intake metadata confirmation and clarification defaults
    29→- `GAP-02` and `GAP-18` rerun and phase re-entry completeness
    30→- `GAP-08` spell and grammar resolution path
    31→- `GAP-19` structured Master CV editor
    32→- `GAP-24` publication persistence and final rendering
    33→- `GAP-06`, `GAP-07`, `GAP-15`, and `GAP-16` review ergonomics, accessibility, and UX cleanup
    34→
    35→This plan does not treat lower-priority persuasion or broad master-data ingestion work as the first implementation target unless they are required by one of the slices above.
    36→
    37→## Success Criteria
    38→
    39→- A user can generate an HTML preview, review and revise layout instructions against that preview, confirm the layout, and then generate final outputs from the confirmed HTML.
    40→- ATS fit is visible throughout customization and generation through a single scoring model with keyword-level status and persisted metadata.
    41→- The ATS score is refreshed at reasonable checkpoints and shown on the same row as `div.position-bar`, aligned to the right edge of the window.
    42→- ATS output uses the expected structural semantics, normalized contact formatting, and hard-vs-soft skill handling.
    43→- Intake includes a confirmation substep for extracted metadata and can preload prior clarification defaults for similar role types.
    44→- Every supported completed stage exposes rerun affordances, preserves prior decisions appropriately, and highlights changed items on re-entry.
    45→- Spell-check behaves as a real quality gate: required review, explicit resolution, and write-back into generated output.
    46→- Core review and generation flows are keyboard-usable, responsive, and easier to navigate on typical laptop screens.
    47→
    48→## Key Decisions
    49→
    50→| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    51→|---|---|---|
    52→| Delivery order | Workflow completeness before UX polish | The main blockers are missing behavior, not only weak presentation |
    53→| Generation contract | Make HTML preview a first-class persisted artifact | Enables layout iteration, diffing, rerun clarity, and final-generation traceability |
    54→| ATS scoring | Compute one shared scoring model used by analysis, review, validation, and finalise | Avoids conflicting numbers across stages |
    55→| ATS score refresh cadence | Refresh after major workflow checkpoints and debounced bursts of review edits, not on every keystroke | Keeps the score useful without creating noisy or distracting UI churn |
    56→| ATS score placement | Render the visible ATS score summary on the same row as `div.position-bar`, right-aligned | Keeps the score globally visible without adding another header band |
    57→| Skill semantics | Introduce hard/soft skill typing in shared data structures, not only in rendering | Needed for both ATS output and UI review logic |
    58→| Rerun behavior | Re-review only changed or new items where possible | Preserves user trust and reduces repeat work |
    59→| Master data scope | Deliver structured editor after the core workflow is dependable | Important, but not the fastest path to fixing the broken applicant journey |
    60→
    61→## Rejected Alternatives
    62→
    63→| Alternative | Why not now |
    64→|---|---|
    65→| Full frontend redesign before gap closure | High churn and weak ROI while workflow contracts are still incomplete |
    66→| ATS score only at finalise time | Too late to guide customization decisions |
    67→| Treat layout review as a modal on top of current generation | Hides state and does not create a durable staged artifact model |
    68→| Build the full natural-language master-data ingestion flow first | Valuable, but lower leverage than fixing generation, ATS, and rerun completeness |
    69→
    70→## Workstreams
    71→
    72→### Workstream 1: Staged Generation Contract
    73→
    74→**Primary gaps:** `GAP-20`, parts of `GAP-05`, `GAP-16`  
    75→**Goal:** Replace the bundled generation flow with explicit preview, layout review, confirmation, and final-generation stages.
    76→
    77→**Tasks**
    78→
    79→1. Audit the current generation, layout, and completion endpoints across `scripts/web_app.py`, `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py`, `web/app.js`, `web/ui-core.js`, and `web/state-manager.js`.
    80→2. Define a persisted generation state model for:
    81→   - preview HTML artifact
    82→   - layout instructions history
    83→   - confirmed preview snapshot
    84→   - final-generation eligibility
    85→3. Add or normalize backend endpoints so preview generation, layout refinement, preview refresh, layout confirmation, and final generation are separate operations with explicit persisted state.
    86→4. Add frontend stage UI for:
    87→   - preview pane
    88→   - layout instruction entry and history
    89→   - preview refresh status
    90→   - confirm-layout action
    91→   - post-confirm final-generation action
    92→5. Carry page-length and generation warnings through preview, layout review, and final validation.
    93→6. Add regression tests for staged generation state transitions in backend, JS, and browser-level workflow coverage.
    94→
    95→**Dependencies**
    96→
    97→- Existing generation and layout APIs
    98→- Session state persistence
    99→- Review-phase navigation and completion state
   100→
   101→**Risks**
   102→
   103→- The current frontend may assume generation is single-step in multiple places.
   104→- Preview artifact persistence could drift from final output generation if the shared contract is weak.
   105→
   106→### Workstream 2: ATS Scoring and ATS Output Semantics
   107→
   108→**Primary gaps:** `GAP-21`, `GAP-22`, `GAP-04`, parts of `GAP-03`, `GAP-10`, `GAP-11`  
   109→**Goal:** Make ATS quality visible during review and consistent in final outputs.
   110→
   111→**Tasks**
   112→
   113→1. Define a shared ATS scoring contract with:
   114→   - overall score
   115→   - hard-requirement weighting
   116→   - matched/missing/bonus keyword states
   117→   - section and match-type provenance
   118→2. Expose the scoring model in analysis/customization UI and refresh it at reasonable intervals:
   119→   - after analysis completes
   120→   - after batches of review decisions or apply actions
   121→   - after rerun completion for affected stages
   122→   - after layout confirmation and final generation
   123→   - after a short debounce window for score-affecting UI actions, rather than on every keystroke
   124→3. Display the ATS score summary on the same row as `div.position-bar`, aligned to the right edge of the window, while preserving responsive behavior for smaller widths.
   125→4. Persist ATS score details into generation metadata and finalise summaries.
   126→5. Normalize ATS DOCX structure, heading levels, contact formatting, employment-header formatting, and date handling to the reviewed story target.
   127→6. Add hard-vs-soft skill typing in shared skill data and use it consistently in UI review, ATS output, and validation.
   128→7. Expand ATS validation so it runs automatically after final generation and includes the missing checks called out in `GAP-04`.
   129→
   130→**Dependencies**
   131→
   132→- Stable generation artifact flow from Workstream 1
   133→- Shared skill schema changes
   134→- Validation and metadata persistence paths
   135→
   136→**Risks**
   137→
   138→- If scoring logic lives in multiple modules, the UI and final metadata will diverge.
   139→- Skill schema changes may affect review tables, generation prompts, validators, and stored metadata.
   140→
   141→### Workstream 3: Intake, Clarification Defaults, and Rerun Completeness
   142→
   143→**Primary gaps:** `GAP-23`, `GAP-02`, `GAP-18`, `GAP-14`  
   144→**Goal:** Make the app dependable for repeated iterative use instead of only clean first-pass runs.
   145→
   146→**Tasks**
   147→
   148→1. Insert an intake-confirmation substep after job extraction and before full analysis.
   149→2. Let the user edit extracted company, role, and date values before analysis proceeds.
   150→3. Persist the session immediately after intake confirmation.
   151→4. Define a role-type matching strategy for prior clarification defaults and preload them with explicit override support.
   152→5. Expose rerun affordances on every supported completed stage.
   153→6. Add changed-item highlighting so reruns only force re-review where content actually changed.
   154→7. Complete layout-stage re-entry so the new staged generation flow supports layout-only refinement without restarting unrelated work.
   155→
   156→**Dependencies**
   157→
   158→- Session metadata persistence
   159→- Stage navigation APIs
   160→- Diffing support for reviewable entities
   161→
   162→**Risks**
   163→
   164→- Poor diffing granularity could cause too much forced re-review and erase the value of rerun support.
   165→- Clarification default reuse can feel unsafe if the role-type matching rule is opaque.
   166→
   167→### Workstream 4: Spell-Check, Publication Persistence, and Output Governance
   168→
   169→**Primary gaps:** `GAP-08`, `GAP-24`, parts of `GAP-03`, `GAP-09`, `GAP-13`  
   170→**Goal:** Close important last-mile quality gaps that affect what actually reaches final output.
   171→
   172→**Tasks**
   173→
   174→1. Make spell-check a blocking review stage until all flagged items are explicitly resolved.
   175→2. Add edit-in-place and skill-name review coverage where the gap analysis says handling is incomplete.
   176→3. Write accepted spell/grammar corrections back into the generated text spans they govern.
   177→4. Persist publication review decisions under the required metadata structure.
   178→5. Ensure final outputs omit the publications section when nothing is selected and render the required heading/count/first-author details when items are selected.
   179→6. Add final-output validation for publication rendering and selected publication metadata.
   180→
   181→**Dependencies**
   182→
   183→- Stable generation artifact model
   184→- Output metadata persistence
   185→- Review decision storage
   186→
   187→**Risks**
   188→
   189→- Write-back logic can silently desynchronize spell audits from generated content if not tied to stable identifiers.
   190→- Publication behavior may differ across HTML preview, PDF, ATS DOCX, and archived metadata if not validated end-to-end.
   191→
   192→### Workstream 5: Structured Master CV Editing
   193→
   194→**Primary gaps:** `GAP-19`, `GAP-01`  
   195→**Goal:** Deliver a real structured maintenance surface for master data without blocking the main workflow repair.
   196→
   197→**Tasks**
   198→
   199→1. Define the editable sections and write-back rules for experiences, skills, education, publications, certifications, and personal info.
   200→2. Build a dedicated Master CV mode separate from the job-specific review flow.
   201→3. Add save safeguards: backup before write, clear diff/changed-state display, and validation before persistence.
   202→4. Align the editor with harvest and approved-skill persistence flows so data contracts do not fork.
   203→
   204→**Dependencies**
   205→
   206→- Shared master-data schema understanding
   207→- Save and backup behavior in `ConversationManager` or related persistence layer
   208→
   209→**Risks**
   210→
   211→- This area can expand quickly into a second product if the scope is not held to structured editing first.
   212→
   213→### Workstream 6: UX, Accessibility, and Review Ergonomics Cleanup
   214→
   215→**Primary gaps:** `GAP-06`, `GAP-07`, `GAP-15`, `GAP-16`  
   216→**Goal:** Improve the daily usability of the now-complete workflow.
   217→
   218→**Tasks**
   219→
   220→1. Add keyboard-friendly sequential rewrite review and preserve diff context while editing.
   221→2. Add row-level reorder controls for experiences, achievements, skills, and publications.
   222→3. Fill `aria-label`, focus-style, and keyboard-operation gaps across icon-only and reorder controls.
   223→4. Reduce shell density, improve long-table behavior, and tune responsive layouts for 1280x800 and smaller widths.
   224→5. Add inline preview/version cues in the places where users compare outputs or re-enter stages.
   225→
   226→**Dependencies**
   227→
   228→- Core workflow stages must be stable enough to polish without rework.
   229→
   230→**Risks**
   231→
   232→- Polishing too early will lead to duplicate work if upstream workflow contracts change.
   233→
   234→## Recommended Delivery Sequence
   235→
   236→### Phase 0: Architecture Audit and Contract Definition — **DONE (2026-03-19)**
   237→
   238→- Confirmed current backend and frontend state for generation, ATS scoring, rerun, spell-check, and master-data flows.
   239→- Produced `tasks/contracts/phase0-contract.md` covering staged generation artifacts, ATS score schema, skill typing, and rerun diffing.
   240→- Identified files and tests to be touched by each workstream.
   241→
   242→### Final Phase: CI Integration & Full-Stack Test Runs
   243→
   244→**Goal:** Ensure full integration tests (backend + UI + persistence + provider‑dependent scenarios) run automatically on main and on a schedule, and that PRs run fast, deterministic suites only.
   245→
   246→**Tasks**
   247→
   248→1. Create a GitHub task/issue to update CI so the full integration suite runs on `push` to `main` and on a nightly schedule. The task should reference the existing PR-focused workflow file and include the desired triggers: `push` (branch: `main`) and `schedule` (daily at 02:00 UTC).
   249→2. Define which tests run where:
   250→   - PRs: `tests/unit`, lint/type checks, and the HTML harness (`npm run test:integration:headless`).
   251→   - Main/nightly: `tests/integration`, end‑to‑end Playwright tests against a live server, and any provider/credentialed scenarios.
   252→3. Add environment gating and secrets guidance to the task: document required secrets (e.g. `LLM_API_KEY`), and ensure integration jobs skip or `pytest.skip(...)` when creds are absent.
   253→4. Implement the workflow changes (or link to the workflow PR) and add a job that starts the web app (`conda` env + `python scripts/web_app.py --llm-provider github`) in the integration runner before running full tests.
   254→5. Add a lightweight monitoring step to report failed test artifacts (logs, pytest xml, playwright trace/video) into the Actions run for debugging.
   255→6. Assign an owner and estimate effort: owner `@team` (or repo maintainer), effort `1-2 days` to get a stable run, `3-5 days` to harden across providers.
   256→
   257→**Risks & Notes**
   258→
   259→- Running provider-dependent tests in CI increases cost and flakiness; prefer using recorded/replay fixtures or mocking where feasible.
   260→- Ensure database/file backups and safe write paths are used in CI to avoid destroying real user data.
   261→- Use the staged approach: land PR-focused workflow first (already present), then implement the `main`/`schedule` expansion tracked by this GitHub task.
   262→
   263→
   264→### Phase 1: Staged Generation Slice — **COMPLETE (2026-03-19)**
   265→
   266→Phase 1 deliverables status:
   267→- [x] Backend endpoints: `/api/cv/generate-preview`, `/api/cv/layout-refine`, `/api/cv/confirm-layout`, `/api/cv/generate-final`, `/api/cv/generation-state` — implemented in `scripts/web_app.py`
   268→- [x] `CVOrchestrator.render_html_preview()` and `generate_final_from_confirmed_html()` — implemented in `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py`
   269→- [x] `generation_state` added to `ConversationManager` state dict and `load_session` backward-compat guards
   270→- [x] `app.session_registry` exposed on Flask app object for test isolation
   271→- [x] Bug fix: `Path("").is_dir()` fallback resolves to cwd — fixed with non-empty string guard
   272→- [x] Regression tests: 26 tests in `tests/test_staged_generation.py` covering all endpoints, guards, happy paths, and session persistence
   273→- [x] Frontend staged generation UI: `layout-instruction.js` has preview pane, instruction loop, confirm button — all wired to new endpoints with legacy fallback
   274→- [x] `completeLayoutReview()` now calls `/api/cv/generate-final` after confirming layout; updates `tabData.cv` with final outputs and refreshes ATS badge
   275→- [x] Generation state synced from backend on session restore: `restoreBackendState()` in `state-manager.js` now calls `/api/cv/generation-state` after restoring other session data
   276→
   277→### Phase 1: Staged Generation Slice
   278→
   279→- Implement Workstream 1 first.
   280→- Goal: unblock the broken `preview -> layout -> final` applicant path.
   281→- Exit criteria: source-verified UI flow exists and is covered by backend and frontend regression tests.
   282→
   283→### Phase 2: ATS Slice — **COMPLETE (2026-03-19)**
   284→
   285→Phase 2 deliverables status:
   286→- [x] `scoring.py`: `_match_status()` tri-state (matched/partial/missing); `match_type` absent for missing keywords, `"exact"` or `"partial"` otherwise; education items extracted from `customizations.education` and fed into `section_matches["education"]`
   287→- [x] `cv_orchestrator.py`: ATS DOCX name paragraph uses `Heading 1` style; `_setup_ats_styles()` configures Heading 1 (16pt, bold, black) fixing the `docx_heading1_present` validation warning
   288→- [x] `app.js`: 3 missing ATS refresh triggers added — after `submitRewriteDecisions`, after `submitSpellCheckDecisions`, and after `generate_cv` completes
   289→- [x] `app.js`: Hard/Soft skill-type badges added to skills review table using `tabData.analysis.required_skills` and `tabData.analysis.nice_to_have_skills`
   290→- [x] Regression tests: 8 new tests in `tests/test_scoring.py` covering tri-state matching, missing keyword `match_type` contract, education section feed, and edge cases; new Heading 1 test in `tests/test_ats_generation.py`
   291→- [x] JS tests: 118/118 pass; Python tests: 73/73 pass for scoring and ATS generation suites
   292→
   293→### Phase 2: ATS Slice
   294→
   295→- Implement Workstream 2 next.
   296→- Goal: make ATS decisions visible before finalisation and align output semantics with stories.
   297→- Exit criteria: one shared ATS score is visible during review, refreshed at reasonable checkpoints, rendered in the `div.position-bar` row, and preserved in generated metadata and final summaries.
   298→
   299→### Phase 3: Iteration and Intake Slice — **COMPLETE (2026-03-19)**
   300→
   301→Phase 3 deliverables status:
   302→- [x] Intake confirmation substep after job extraction — implemented in `scripts/web_app.py` (`intake_metadata`, `confirm_intake`)
   303→- [x] Prior clarification defaults with role-type matching and explicit override — implemented in `scripts/web_app.py` (`prior_clarifications`)
   304→- [x] Session persisted immediately after intake confirmation
   305→- [x] Spell-check and generate re-run affordances exposed
   306→
   307→### Phase 4: Quality-Gate Slice — **COMPLETE (2026-03-19)**
   308→
   309→Phase 4 deliverables status:
   310→- [x] Spell-check blocking guard — requires explicit resolution before proceeding
   311→- [x] Publication decisions persistence — decisions stored in session and respected by final output
   312→- [x] Write-back of accepted spell/grammar corrections integrated
   313→
   314→### Phase 5: Master Data Editing Slice — **COMPLETE (2026-03-21)**
   315→
   316→- Implement Workstream 5.
   317→- Goal: provide structured master-data maintenance once the main applicant workflow is stable.
   318→
   319→Phase 5 deliverables status:
   320→- [x] Backup-before-write safeguard for `Master_CV_Data.json`
   321→- [x] Pre-save structural validation for master-data writes
   322→- [x] Clear changed-state display in the Master CV editor after save/delete actions
   323→- [x] `scripts/utils/master_data_validator.py` — `ValidationResult` dataclass + `validate_master_data()` / `validate_master_data_file()` helpers
   324→- [x] `schemas/master_cv_data.schema.json` — JSON Schema 2020-12 for master data (experience, skills, education, awards, personal_info)
   325→- [x] `MASTER_CV_DATA_SPECIFICATION.md` — human-readable field reference
   326→- [x] `scripts/validate_master_data.py` — CLI wrapper, exit 0/1
   327→- [x] Pre-load validation in `CVOrchestrator._load_master_data()` and `generate_cv.load_master_data()`
   328→- [x] `GET /api/master-data/validate` endpoint
   329→- [x] `POST /api/master-data/preview-diff` endpoint (read-only before/after diff for personal_info and skill sections)
   330→- [x] 53 Python tests (TestMasterDataPreviewDiff 17, TestValidateMasterData 12, TestMasterDataValidateEndpoint 3, TestMasterDataOverview 5, + existing)
   331→
   332→### Phase 6: UX and Accessibility Hardening — **COMPLETE (2026-03-20)**
   333→
   334→Phase 6 deliverables status:
   335→- [x] Row-level reorder controls for review tables implemented in the split frontend modules (experience, achievements, skills, publications)
   336→- [x] Icon-only review actions now include `aria-label` coverage in active review surfaces
   337→- [x] Review-table rendering hardened around explicit re-render helpers and ordering state persistence
   338→- [x] Layout review usability improved with persisted base-font-size control and immediate preview re-render loop
   339→
   340→- Implement Workstream 6.
   341→- Goal: reduce friction and finish keyboard/responsive coverage after the core flows stop moving.
   342→
   343→### Refactor Timing
   344→
   345→- Do not execute the full refactor program before Phase 1.
   346→- Use targeted refactoring only where it directly enables:
   347→  - staged generation contract work
   348→  - ATS score calculation and header-row display
   349→  - rerun/highlighting state ownership
   350→- Revisit the broader refactor candidates after Phase 1 and again after Phase 2, when the new workflow and ATS contracts are stable enough to split safely.
   351→- Treat `web/layout-instruction.js`, `web/ui-core.js`, and `web/state-manager.js` cleanup as follow-on work unless a narrow extraction is needed to land the staged-generation slice.
   352→
   353→## Task Breakdown With Dependencies
   354→
   355→| ID | Task | Depends on | Effort | Risk |
   356→|---|---|---|---|---|
   357→| T1 | Audit current generation/layout contract | none | M | M |
   358→| T2 | Define persisted preview and layout state model | T1 | M | H |
   359→| T3 | Split backend generation into preview, layout refine, confirm, final | T2 | L | H |
   360→| T4 | Build frontend staged generation UI | T3 | L | H |
   361→| T5 | Add generation state regression tests | T3, T4 | M | M |
   362→| T6 | Define shared ATS score and keyword-status schema | T1 | M | H |
   363→| T7 | Implement ATS score recalculation and UI surfacing | T6 | L | H |
   364→| T8 | Normalize ATS output structure and skill typing | T6 | L | H |
   365→| T9 | Auto-run ATS validation after final generation | T3, T8 | M | M |
   366→| T10 | Add intake confirmation and metadata persistence | T1 | M | M |
   367→| T11 | Add clarification-default preload logic | T10 | M | M |
   368→| T12 | Expose rerun affordances and changed-item highlighting | T3, T10 | L | H |
   369→| T13 | Make spell-check blocking and write-back capable | T3 | L | H |
   370→| T14 | Persist publication decisions through final outputs | T3 | M | M |
   371→| T15 | Build structured Master CV editor | T1 | XL | M |
   372→| T16 | Improve rewrite ergonomics, reorder UI, and accessibility | T4, T12 | L | M |
   373→
   374→## Validation Strategy
   375→
   376→- Add backend tests for staged generation transitions, ATS metadata persistence, rerun state, spell-check write-back, and publication rendering rules.
   377→- Add JS tests for new state-management helpers, ATS score display logic and refresh cadence, rerun highlighting, and accessibility-sensitive controls.
   378→- Add UI coverage that the ATS score remains visible in the `div.position-bar` row and degrades cleanly on narrower layouts.
   379→- Add browser-level workflow coverage for:
   380→  - intake confirmation
   381→  - staged generation
   382→  - layout refinement loop
   383→  - rerun from completed stages
   384→  - spell-check completion gating
   385→  - publication omission and rendering behavior
   386→- Re-run the source-verification review slices after each major phase instead of waiting for the end.
   387→
   388→## Suggested First Slice
   389→
   390→Start with a thin vertical slice that closes the highest-leverage blocker:
   391→
   392→1. Define persisted preview/layout state.
   393→2. Split the generation API into preview, layout refresh, confirm, and final.
   394→3. Add a minimal frontend preview pane plus layout instruction loop.
   395→4. Add one browser test that proves `generate preview -> revise layout -> confirm -> final generate`.
   396→
   397→This slice should make `GAP-20` materially smaller while setting up the contracts needed by ATS validation, rerun completeness, and final-output governance.
   398→
   399→## Open Questions
   400→
   401→- Should the confirmed HTML preview become the canonical source for both PDF and ATS DOCX generation, or only for PDF/layout-sensitive outputs?
   402→- How much of the Master CV editor should be delivered before NL-driven update and document-ingestion work are tackled?
   403→- Is Google Drive sync still in scope for the near-term finalise slice, or should it remain deferred while the core workflow is repaired?
   404→
   405→## Recommendation
   406→
   407→Approve Phase 0 and Phase 1 immediately, using only enabling refactors during those slices, then revisit the broader refactor backlog after the staged generation slice lands. That keeps the team focused on the broken applicant workflow first while preserving the option to narrow or expand later structural work based on what the new generation contract exposes.
   408→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./tasks/refactor-candidates-2026-03-18.md`

```text
     1→# Refactoring Candidates Review
     2→
     3→Date: 2026-03-18
     4→Scope: Python and JavaScript source in `scripts/` and `web/`
     5→
     6→## Executive Summary
     7→
     8→The highest-value refactoring work is concentrated in two orchestration files:
     9→
    10→- `scripts/web_app.py` centralizes Flask app setup, dependency wiring, request locking, route registration, request parsing, and session-state mutation inside one oversized app factory.
    11→- `web/app.js` centralizes chat flow, session restore, workflow progression, post-analysis Q&A, review interactions, CV editor rendering, and download-stage UI in one browser-global script.
    12→
    13→Those two files dominate maintenance risk because they mix unrelated responsibilities and own cross-cutting workflow state. The next tier of candidates are `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py`, `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py`, `scripts/utils/llm_client.py`, and `web/ui-core.js`, each of which contains smaller but still materially complex blocks that would become easier to change once the primary orchestration files are split.
    14→
    15→## Method
    16→
    17→- Measured file size using source line counts.
    18→- Measured Python block complexity with an AST-based control-flow heuristic.
    19→- Measured JavaScript size with file-level metrics and then validated candidate blocks by direct source inspection.
    20→- Excluded `.claude/`, caches, logs, generated artifacts, and dependency directories.
    21→
    22→Notes:
    23→
    24→- Python block complexity values below are approximate but directionally reliable.
    25→- JavaScript block complexity in `web/app.js` was validated manually because an automated brace-based detector over-expanded nested blocks in that file.
    26→
    27→## Ranked Candidates
    28→
    29→| Priority | Area | Candidate | Size | Complexity Signal | Risk | Refactoring Benefit | Recommended Direction |
    30→| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
    31→| 1 | Python | `scripts/web_app.py` / `create_app()` and nested route handlers | 4,063 LOC file; `create_app()` spans lines 309-3837 (3,529 lines) | App factory owns dependency setup, lock lifecycle, route registration, request parsing, and workflow transitions; nested handlers such as `fetch_job_url()` at lines 1599-1837 add more special-case logic | Very High | Highest reduction in blast radius; makes route behavior testable without booting the whole app; easier lock/state auditing | Split into blueprint-style route modules plus service helpers for URL ingestion, session APIs, generation, and auth |
    32→| 2 | JavaScript | `web/app.js` monolith | 8,308 LOC file | Verified clusters: init and listeners (738-814), message dispatch (1649-1784), post-analysis Q&A UI (2155-2419), CV editor rendering (2874-3195), plus many more workflow functions in one global namespace | Very High | Largest frontend maintainability gain; untangles workflow state, DOM rendering, and transport concerns | Extract modules for workflow controller, chat/message routing, analysis questions, review flows, CV editor, and download/finalize |
    33→| 3 | Python | `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py` selection/generation/validation cluster | 2,529 LOC file; `_select_content_hybrid()` lines 1117-1348 (232 lines); `validate_ats_report()` lines 2185-2529 (345 lines) | File mixes content selection, output generation, ATS validation, PDF/DOCX handling, and publication logic | High | Clearer ownership boundaries; lower regression risk in generation pipeline; easier targeted tests | Separate selectors, render/generation steps, and ATS validation into distinct collaborators |
    34→| 4 | Python | `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py` / `_execute_action()` | 1,533 LOC file; `_execute_action()` lines 458-733 (276 lines) | Large action dispatcher with embedded phase transitions, data repair, recommendation generation, decision application, and CV generation | High | Simplifies workflow changes; reduces action-specific coupling and conditional drift | Replace the `if/elif` dispatcher with per-action command handlers or a dispatch map |
    35→| 5 | Python | `scripts/utils/llm_client.py` prompt-building and response-parsing cluster | 2,245 LOC file; `recommend_customizations()` lines 159-352; `rank_publications_for_job()` lines 1142-1289; `_propose_rewrites_via_chat()` lines 1291-1426 | File combines provider abstraction, prompt construction, JSON parsing, rewrite filtering, and ranking logic | Medium-High | Easier prompt iteration, stronger validation, lower provider-specific regression risk | Extract prompt builders and response parsers into dedicated helpers; keep providers focused on transport |
    36→| 6 | JavaScript | `web/ui-core.js` tab/modal/model-selection controller | 1,118 LOC file; `setupEventListeners()` lines 236-318; `_buildModelTable()` lines 821-975 | UI shell mixes accessibility, tab routing, modal control, provider/model catalog rendering, DataTables integration, and pricing refresh | Medium | Better UI cohesion and smaller test surface; less coupling between tab logic and model selection | Extract model modal/table code into its own module and keep `ui-core.js` as stage/navigation shell |
    37→| 7 | JavaScript | `web/state-manager.js` backend/localStorage/session restoration ownership | 332 LOC file; `restoreSession()` and `restoreBackendState()` lines 166-295 | Module is not huge, but it overlaps with session/state behavior still present in `web/app.js`, creating split ownership | Medium | Removes duplicated restore/save logic and makes resume behavior predictable | Make this the single owner of browser state hydration and session restoration; have `app.js` consume state instead of reconstructing it |
    38→| 8 | JavaScript | `web/layout-instruction.js` layout review UI | 329 LOC file; `initiateLayoutInstructions()` lines 15-78; `submitLayoutInstruction()` lines 105-155 | Moderate complexity with dynamic DOM creation, history management, inline `prompt()`-based clarification, and partial undo | Low-Medium | Good cleanup target after larger splits; improves layout-review testability | Convert to a small view-model module and replace inline DOM string assembly with focused render helpers |
    39→
    40→## Detailed Notes
    41→
    42→### 1. `scripts/web_app.py`
    43→
    44→Primary concern:
    45→
    46→- `create_app()` is no longer an app factory in the narrow sense. It acts as a dependency container, route registry, request-lock manager, and controller layer for most user workflows.
    47→
    48→Evidence:
    49→
    50→- App setup, auth, `CVOrchestrator`, and `ConversationManager` are all instantiated inside `create_app()`.
    51→- Nested handlers such as `fetch_job_url()` contain domain-specific scraping rules, validation, HTML extraction, state mutation, and error shaping in one route function.
    52→
    53→Refactor shape:
    54→
    55→- Keep `create_app()` limited to configuration, dependency creation, and blueprint registration.
    56→- Move route logic into cohesive modules by domain: status/session APIs, job ingestion, generation/download, auth/model catalog, and harvest/finalize.
    57→- Keep the single-session lock, but centralize exempt-path policy and lock handling in a dedicated wrapper.
    58→
    59→### 2. `web/app.js`
    60→
    61→Primary concern:
    62→
    63→- The file has become the frontend runtime for nearly the whole application, with browser globals as the integration mechanism.
    64→
    65→Evidence from inspected blocks:
    66→
    67→- `init()` and `setupEventListeners()` combine startup sequencing, local persistence, restore behavior, and button wiring.
    68→- `_handleLLMMessage()` combines transport, retry UI, JSON extraction, customization detection, and fallback rendering.
    69→- `askPostAnalysisQuestions()` through `submitAllAnswers()` mixes persistence, rendering, AI drafting, validation, and workflow transitions.
    70→- `populateCVEditorTab()` through `renderExperienceCards()` combine data fetching, templating, event routing, mutation, and persistence cues.
    71→
    72→Refactor shape:
    73→
    74→- Keep a thin top-level bootstrap.
    75→- Extract modules for: session bootstrapping, chat transport/message routing, post-analysis questions, review flows, CV editor, and generation/download orchestration.
    76→- Replace window-global coordination with explicit state and module APIs.
    77→
    78→### 3. `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py`
    79→
    80→Primary concern:
    81→
    82→- The file combines three different kinds of work: business rules for content selection, output generation, and post-generation validation.
    83→
    84→Evidence:
    85→
    86→- `_select_content_hybrid()` contains omission/recommendation rules, skills-shape compatibility handling, scoring, bullet reordering, summary resolution, and publication filtering.
    87→- `validate_ats_report()` contains 16 checks covering DOCX, HTML/JSON-LD, WeasyPrint rendering, PDF page size, and page-count policy.
    88→
    89→Refactor shape:
    90→
    91→- Extract a content-selector service.
    92→- Extract ATS validation into a standalone validator object or module.
    93→- Keep orchestration methods focused on sequence and data flow.
    94→
    95→### 4. `scripts/utils/conversation_manager.py`
    96→
    97→Primary concern:
    98→
    99→- `_execute_action()` encodes most workflow branching in one imperative block.
   100→
   101→Evidence:
   102→
   103→- It handles analysis, question generation, recommendation generation, decision normalization, summary/session injection, publication decisions, and final generation.
   104→- It also repairs persisted JSON strings inline, which makes action logic harder to reason about.
   105→
   106→Refactor shape:
   107→
   108→- Introduce action-specific handlers with a common interface.
   109→- Move state normalization into dedicated helpers called before dispatch.
   110→- Keep phase transitions explicit and centrally validated.
   111→
   112→### 5. `scripts/utils/llm_client.py`
   113→
   114→Primary concern:
   115→
   116→- The file is doing transport-layer work and prompt-architecture work at the same time.
   117→
   118→Evidence:
   119→
   120→- `recommend_customizations()` builds a large provider-agnostic prompt, includes user preferences and conversation history formatting, and also performs backward-compatibility normalization.
   121→- `rank_publications_for_job()` and `_propose_rewrites_via_chat()` repeat a similar pattern: compact serialization, prompt construction, JSON parsing, filtering, and fallback shaping.
   122→
   123→Refactor shape:
   124→
   125→- Extract reusable prompt-builder classes/functions.
   126→- Extract schema validation and normalization helpers.
   127→- Keep provider classes focused on API transport and capability selection.
   128→
   129→### 6. `web/ui-core.js`
   130→
   131→Primary concern:
   132→
   133→- `ui-core.js` is better factored than `web/app.js`, but it still owns too many unrelated UI concerns.
   134→
   135→Evidence:
   136→
   137→- `setupEventListeners()` handles tabs, keyboard behavior, ESC modal closing, chat toggling, and dialog background clicks.
   138→- `_buildModelTable()` manages header generation, DataTable lifecycle, row rendering, filtering, and selection synchronization.
   139→
   140→Refactor shape:
   141→
   142→- Split navigation/modal shell from model catalog/table behavior.
   143→- Keep accessibility utilities in a separate shared module if they are reused.
   144→
   145→### 7. `web/state-manager.js`
   146→
   147→Primary concern:
   148→
   149→- This module should be the single state owner, but app-level restore/save logic is still distributed across `web/app.js`.
   150→
   151→Evidence:
   152→
   153→- `restoreSession()` and `restoreBackendState()` are already substantial.
   154→- The current structure suggests state concerns are only partially centralized.
   155→
   156→Refactor shape:
   157→
   158→- Make this module authoritative for localStorage, backend hydration, and phase restoration.
   159→- Have higher-level UI modules subscribe to or request state rather than rebuilding it.
   160→
   161→### 8. `web/layout-instruction.js`
   162→
   163→Primary concern:
   164→
   165→- This is not a crisis file, but it has enough view logic and state mutation to justify cleanup after the main hotspots.
   166→
   167→Evidence:
   168→
   169→- The module dynamically creates the whole layout panel, binds listeners, stores history in globals, and uses `prompt()` for clarification.
   170→- `undoInstruction()` is explicitly partial and relies on re-render placeholders rather than a real model.
   171→
   172→Refactor shape:
   173→
   174→- Treat layout review as a self-contained feature module with its own render and state helpers.
   175→
   176→## Recommended Order
   177→
   178→This order is the recommended refactor backlog order, not the recommended execution order relative to the March 19 UI-gap implementation work.
   179→
   180→For near-term execution:
   181→
   182→1. During UI-gap Phase 0 and Phase 1, do only enabling refactors that directly support staged generation, ATS score surfacing, and rerun/state ownership.
   183→2. After the staged generation slice stabilizes, begin the broader structural refactors below.
   184→3. Reassess after the ATS slice before taking on the largest frontend/backend splits in full.
   185→
   186→Broader refactor backlog order:
   187→
   188→1. Split `scripts/web_app.py` into app factory plus route/service modules.
   189→2. Split `web/app.js` into a bootstrap and feature modules.
   190→3. Separate selection, rendering, and ATS validation concerns in `scripts/utils/cv_orchestrator.py`.
   191→4. Replace `ConversationManager._execute_action()` with dispatched handlers.
   192→5. Extract prompt builders/parsers from `scripts/utils/llm_client.py`.
   193→6. Extract model catalog UI from `web/ui-core.js`.
   194→7. Make `web/state-manager.js` the single browser state owner.
   195→8. Clean up `web/layout-instruction.js` after the larger workflow splits land.
   196→
   197→## OBO Session
   198→
   199→An OBO session has been created for these recommendations:
   200→
   201→- `session_20260318_refactor_review.json`
```

```text
---
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Minimal Web UI for LLM-Driven CV Generator

Serves a single-page web app with endpoints to:
- Submit a job description
- Send chat messages to the assistant
- View current state
- Save session

Run:
    python scripts/web_app.py
```

_**User (2026-03-21 14:17:28Z)**_

# Authorize Agent Access

Authorize the requested folder path or shell command across all supported agents.

## Step 1 — Classify the request

Determine from the user's request or argument:
- **Folder path**: an absolute or `~/`-prefixed directory path
- **Command**: a shell command prefix (e.g. `git rebase`, `npm run build`, `./deploy.sh`)
- **Both**: if the user mentioned both

If the argument is ambiguous, ask: "Is this a folder path, a shell command, or both?"

## Step 2 — Edit `~/src/vscode-config/settings.json` (Copilot + Cline)

Read the file first, then add the authorization.

**For a folder**, add to `"chat.tools.fileSystem.autoApprove"`:
```json
"/absolute/path/*": true
```

**For a command**, add to `"chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove"`:
```json
"command prefix": true
```

## Step 3 — Edit `~/.claude/settings.json` (Claude Code)

Read the file first, then add the authorization.

**For a folder**, add the path to `"permissions.additionalDirectories"`:
```json
"/absolute/path"
```

**For a command**, add a Bash pattern to `"permissions.allow"`:
```json
"Bash(command prefix:*)"
```

## Step 4 — Run setup.sh to propagate

```bash
cd ~/src/vscode-config && ./setup.sh
```

## Step 5 — Verify and report

1. Confirm both JSON files are valid syntax
2. Report what was added to each file
3. Remind the user to reload VS Code window (`Cmd+Shift+P → Developer: Reload Window`) and restart any Claude Code sessions

Follow the full procedural guide in `#authorize-agent` for edge cases, pattern syntax, and troubleshooting.


ARGUMENTS: wc

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-03-21 14:18:44Z)**_

You are working in the cv-builder project. Your task is to refactor `scripts/web_app.py` by splitting the Flask app factory into cohesive route/blueprint modules, per the refactoring plan in `tasks/refactor-candidates-2026-03-18.md`.

## IMPORTANT: Branch Setup
After the worktree is created, ensure you are on the `devel` branch:
```
git checkout devel
```

## Goal

`scripts/web_app.py` is 5953 lines. `create_app()` spans lines 309–5650+ and acts as a dependency container, route registry, request-lock manager, and controller layer for most user workflows.

**Target state:** `create_app()` is limited to:
- Configuration loading
- Dependency creation (orchestrator, conversation manager, session registry, auth)
- Blueprint registration
- Lock/middleware setup

Route logic lives in cohesive **Flask blueprint modules** under `scripts/routes/`.

## Route Modules to Create

Create `scripts/routes/__init__.py` (empty) and the following blueprint modules:

### 1. `scripts/routes/static_routes.py`
Handles: index, favicon, static web files, logo
Routes: `/`, `/favicon.ico`, `/web/<path:filename>`, `/logo`

### 2. `scripts/routes/session_routes.py`  
Handles: session management — list, load, save, delete, rename, trash operations, new/claim/takeover/active/evict sessions, positions, reset, history
Routes: `/api/save`, `/api/list-sessions`, `/api/load-items`, `/api/load-session`, `/api/delete-session`, `/api/list-trash`, `/api/trash-restore`, `/api/trash-delete-one`, `/api/trash-empty`, `/api/rename-current`, `/api/rename-session`, `/api/positions`, `/api/set-position`, `/api/reset`, `/api/history`, `/api/sessions/new`, `/api/sessions/claim`, `/api/sessions/takeover`, `/api/sessions/active`, `/api/sessions/<session_id>/evict`

### 3. `scripts/routes/status_routes.py`
Handles: status, context stats, update generation settings, post-analysis responses, post-analysis questions, draft response, intake metadata, confirm intake, prior clarifications
Routes: `/api/status`, `/api/context-stats`, `/api/update-generation-settings`, `/api/post-analysis-responses`, `/api/post-analysis-questions`, `/api/post-analysis-draft-response`, `/api/intake-metadata`, `/api/confirm-intake`, `/api/prior-clarifications`

### 4. `scripts/routes/job_routes.py`
Handles: job submission, URL fetch, file upload, job file load, send message, do action, back to phase, re-run phase
Routes: `/api/submit-job`, `/api/fetch-job-url`, `/api/upload`, `/api/load-job-file`, `/api/send-message`, `/api/do-action`, `/api/back-to-phase`, `/api/re-run-phase`

### 5. `scripts/routes/review_routes.py`
Handles: review decisions, achievement edits, rewrites, publications, reorder, synonyms, spell check, layout review, ATS, persuasion
Routes: `/api/save-review-decisions`, `/api/save-achievement-edits`, `/api/rewrite-achievement`, `/api/rewrite-achievement-outcome`, `/api/get-cv-data`, `/api/save-cv-data`, `/api/get-rewrites`, `/api/approve-rewrites`, `/api/publication-recommendations`, `/api/reorder-bullets`, `/api/proposed-bullet-order`, `/api/reorder-rows`, `/api/synonym-lookup`, `/api/synonym-map`, `/api/get-experience-details`, `/api/spell-check-sections`, `/api/spell-check-text`, `/api/custom-dictionary`, `/api/custom-dictionary/add`, `/api/spell-check-complete`, `/api/get-layout-html`, `/api/apply-layout-instruction`, `/api/get-layout-history`, `/api/complete-layout-review`, `/api/update-layout-settings`, `/api/ats-validate`, `/api/persuasion-check`

### 6. `scripts/routes/generation_routes.py`
Handles: CV generation, staged generation, download, finalise, harvest
Routes: `/api/cv/generate-preview`, `/api/cv/layout-refine`, `/api/cv/confirm-layout`, `/api/cv/generate-final`, `/api/cv/generation-state`, `/api/cv/ats-score`, `/api/generate-professional-summary`, `/api/update-generation-settings`, `/download/<path:filename>`, `/api/finalise`, `/api/harvest-candidates`, `/api/harvest-apply`

### 7. `scripts/routes/auth_routes.py`
Handles: Copilot auth, model catalog, model get/set/test, pricing
Routes: `/api/auth/copilot/start`, `/api/auth/copilot/poll`, `/api/auth/copilot/status`, `/api/auth/copilot/logout`, `/api/model`, `/api/model-catalog`, `/api/model-catalog/refresh-pricing`, `/api/set-model`, `/api/test-model`

### 8. `scripts/routes/master_data_routes.py`
Handles: master data CRUD, cover letter, screening questions
Routes: `/api/master-data/*`, `/api/cover-letter/*`, `/api/screening/*`

## Pattern for Blueprint Modules

Each blueprint module should follow this pattern:

```python
"""
<Description of what this blueprint handles>
"""
from flask import Blueprint, jsonify, request, ...

bp = Blueprint('<name>', __name__)

# Helper functions used only by this blueprint go here

@bp.route('/api/...', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
def some_route():
    ...
```

## Dependency Injection

The routes need access to shared dependencies (session registry, orchestrator factory, conversation manager, request lock, etc.). Use a pattern where dependencies are injected at blueprint registration time via a factory function:

```python
# In scripts/routes/session_routes.py
def create_blueprint(deps):
    bp = Blueprint('session', __name__)
    
    @bp.route('/api/save', methods=['POST'])
    def save():
        session = deps['get_session']()
        ...
    
    return bp
```

Or alternatively, use Flask's `g` object and `before_request` hooks, or pass a dependency container object.

**Recommended approach**: Use a `create_blueprint(deps)` factory pattern where `deps` is a dict or object containing:
- `get_session` — callable for getting current session
- `session_registry` — the SessionRegistry instance
- `build_objects` — callable to create orchestrator/conversation_manager for a session
- `request_lock` — threading.Lock instance
- `app_config` — config dict
- `exempt_from_lock` — set of paths exempt from the lock

## Existing Helper Functions

Several module-level helpers exist in `web_app.py` that need to stay accessible:
- `_catalog_provider_api_key()`, `_catalog_provider_api_base()`, `_catalog_normalize_model_id()`, `_catalog_discover_provider_models()`, `_get_available_models()`, `_refresh_dynamic_model_cache()`, `_maybe_refresh_dynamic_cache_in_background()` → move to `scripts/routes/auth_routes.py` or a new `scripts/utils/model_catalog.py`
- `_text_similarity()` → move to a shared utils location
- `_web_app_build_objects()` → keep in `web_app.py` as a factory helper
- `_load_master()`, `_extract_year()`, `_validate_master_for_persistence()`, `_save_master()` → move to `scripts/routes/master_data_routes.py`
- `_compile_harvest_candidates()`, `_harvest_apply_bullet()`, `_harvest_add_skill()`, `_harvest_add_summary_variant()` → move to `scripts/routes/generation_routes.py`

## The Refactored create_app()

After splitting, `create_app()` should look roughly like:

```python
def create_app(args) -> Flask:
    app = Flask(__name__, static_folder=None)
    # ... config setup ...
    
    # Create shared dependencies
    session_registry = SessionRegistry(...)
    request_lock = threading.Lock()
    auth_manager = AuthManager(...)
    
    deps = {
        'session_registry': session_registry,
        'request_lock': request_lock,
        'get_session': lambda required=True: _get_session(session_registry, required),
        'build_objects': lambda entry: _build_objects_for_registry(entry),
        ...
    }
    
    # Register blueprints
    from scripts.routes import static_routes, session_routes, ...
    app.register_blueprint(static_routes.create_blueprint(deps))
    app.register_blueprint(session_routes.create_blueprint(deps))
    ...
    
    # Before/after request hooks
    @app.before_request
    def acquire_lock():
        ...
    
    return app
```

## Steps

1. Read `scripts/web_app.py` carefully, especially `create_app()`, to understand all routes and nested helpers
2. Create `scripts/routes/__init__.py`
3. Create each blueprint module, moving routes and their helpers
4. Update `create_app()` in `web_app.py` to register blueprints and strip out moved code
5. Ensure `app.session_registry` is still exposed (used by tests)
6. Run the existing test suite to verify nothing is broken:
   ```bash
   cd /path/to/worktree
   conda run -n cv-builder python -m pytest tests/ -x -q 2>&1 | head -100
   ```
7. Fix any import or routing issues revealed by tests

## Constraints

- Do NOT change any route URLs (the frontend depends on exact URL strings)
- Do NOT change any response formats or status codes
- Do NOT change the behavior of any route — only restructure where code lives
- The `request_lock` and lock-exempt paths must work exactly as before
- Keep `parse_args()`, `main()`, and `_env_file_has_value()` in `web_app.py`
- Keep all dataclasses (`StatusResponse`, `SessionItem`, etc.) in `web_app.py` or a shared `scripts/utils/web_types.py`

## Test Run

After completing the refactor, run:
```bash
conda run -n cv-builder python -m pytest tests/ -q --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/refactor_test_output.txt
head -80 /tmp/refactor_test_output.txt
tail -20 /tmp/refactor_test_output.txt
```

Report the test results clearly.

## Output

When done, report:
1. What blueprint modules were created and how many routes each contains
2. The final line count of `web_app.py` after splitting
3. Test pass/fail counts
4. Any issues encountered and how they were resolved

```text
     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Minimal Web UI for LLM-Driven CV Generator
     4→
     5→Serves a single-page web app with endpoints to:
     6→- Submit a job description
     7→- Send chat messages to the assistant

     9→- Save session
    10→
    11→Run:
    12→    python scripts/web_app.py

    14→Then open http://localhost:5001
    15→"""
    16→
    17→import argparse

    19→import dataclasses
    20→import json

    40→env_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / '.env'
    41→if env_path.exists():
    42→    load_dotenv(env_path)
    43→
    44→# Ensure scripts are importable
    45→sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent))
    46→
    47→from utils.config import get_config, validate_config, ConfigurationError
    48→from utils.llm_client import get_llm_provider, PROVIDER_MODELS, PROVIDER_BILLING, MODEL_INFO

    55→    refresh_pricing_cache, maybe_refresh_in_background, lookup_runtime_pricing_bulk, STATIC_PRICING,

    62→
    63→
    64→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    65→# API response DTOs — typed dataclasses for the most critical endpoints.
    66→#
    67→# Using dataclasses ensures:
    68→#   • All fields are always present in the JSON response (no silent omissions).
    69→#   • Shape changes (field additions / renames) surface as TypeError at the call site.
    70→#   • The expected response contract is documented in one place.
    71→#
    72→# JS mirror validators live in web/app.js (parseStatusResponse, etc.).
    73→# Update both sides together when adding/removing fields.
    74→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→
    76→@dataclass
    77→class StatusResponse:
    78→    """Response shape for GET /api/status."""
    79→    position_name: Optional[str]
    80→    phase: Optional[str]
    81→    llm_provider: str
    82→    llm_model: Optional[str]
    83→    job_description: bool
    84→    job_description_text: Optional[str]
    85→    job_analysis: Optional[Dict[str, Any]]
    86→    post_analysis_questions: List[Any]
    87→    post_analysis_answers: Dict[str, Any]
    88→    customizations: Optional[Dict[str, Any]]
    89→    generated_files: Optional[Dict[str, Any]]
    90→    generation_progress: List[Any]
    91→    persuasion_warnings: List[Any]
    92→    all_experience_ids: List[str]
    93→    all_experiences: List[Dict[str, Any]]
    94→    all_skills: List[Any]
    95→    all_achievements: List[Any]
    96→    professional_summaries: Dict[str, Any]
    97→    copilot_auth: Dict[str, Any]
    98→    iterating: bool
    99→    reentry_phase: Optional[str]
   100→    experience_decisions: Dict[str, Any]
   101→    skill_decisions: Dict[str, Any]
   102→    achievement_decisions: Dict[str, Any]
   103→    publication_decisions: Dict[str, Any]
   104→    summary_focus_override: Optional[str]
   105→    extra_skills: List[Any]
   106→    extra_skill_matches: Dict[str, List[str]]
   107→    session_file: str
   108→    max_skills: int
   109→    achievement_edits: Dict[str, Any]
   110→    intake: Dict[str, Any]
   111→
   112→
   113→@dataclass
   114→class SessionItem:
   115→    """One entry in the SessionListResponse."""
   116→    path: str
   117→    position_name: str
   118→    timestamp: str
   119→    phase: str
   120→    has_job: bool
   121→    has_analysis: bool
   122→    has_customizations: bool
   123→
   124→
   125→@dataclass
   126→class SessionListResponse:
   127→    """Response shape for GET /api/sessions."""

   129→
   130→    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   131→        return {"sessions": [dataclasses.asdict(s) for s in self.sessions]}

   158→
   159→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   160→# Model-catalog helpers (module-level for testability)
   161→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   162→
   163→_CATALOG_LIST_MODELS_CAPABLE: set[str] = {"openai", "anthropic", "gemini", "groq"}
   164→_CATALOG_STATIC_ONLY: set[str] = {"copilot-oauth", "copilot", "github", "local"}
   165→
   166→
   167→def _catalog_provider_api_key(provider: str) -> Optional[str]:
   168→    if provider == "openai":
   169→        return os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
   170→    if provider == "anthropic":
   171→        return os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
   172→    if provider == "gemini":
   173→        return os.getenv("GEMINI_API_KEY") or os.getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY")
   174→    if provider == "groq":
   175→        return os.getenv("GROQ_API_KEY")
   176→    return None
   177→
   178→
   179→def _catalog_provider_api_base(provider: str) -> Optional[str]:
   180→    if provider == "openai":
   181→        return os.getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL")
   182→    if provider == "anthropic":
   183→        return os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL")
   184→    if provider == "gemini":
   185→        return os.getenv("GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL")
   186→    if provider == "groq":
   187→        return os.getenv("GROQ_BASE_URL")
   188→    return None
   189→
   190→
   191→def _catalog_normalize_model_id(model_id: str) -> str:
   192→    """Normalize Gemini-style 'models/gemini-2.5-flash' IDs to bare model names."""
   193→    if model_id.startswith("models/"):
   194→        return model_id.split("/", 1)[1]
   195→    return model_id
   196→
   197→
   198→def _catalog_discover_provider_models(provider: str) -> Optional[List[str]]:
   199→    """Return runtime model list for providers that support list_models, or None."""
   200→    if provider not in _CATALOG_LIST_MODELS_CAPABLE or provider in _CATALOG_STATIC_ONLY:
   201→        return None
   202→
   203→    api_key = _catalog_provider_api_key(provider)
   204→    if not api_key:
   205→        return None
   206→
   207→    try:
   208→        from any_llm import list_models as anyllm_list_models
   209→    except Exception:
   210→        return None
   211→
   212→    try:
   213→        kwargs: Dict[str, Any] = {
   214→            "provider": provider,
   215→            "api_key":  api_key,
   216→        }
   217→        api_base = _catalog_provider_api_base(provider)
   218→        if api_base:
   219→            kwargs["api_base"] = api_base
   220→
   221→        models = anyllm_list_models(**kwargs)
   222→        names: List[str] = []
   223→        for item in models:
   224→            model_id = getattr(item, "id", None)
   225→            if model_id is None:
   226→                model_id = str(item)
   227→            model_id = _catalog_normalize_model_id(str(model_id))
   228→            if model_id:
   229→                names.append(model_id)
   230→
   231→        # Keep order stable and deduplicate.
   232→        unique: List[str] = []
   233→        seen: set[str] = set()
   234→        for name in names:
   235→            if name in seen:
   236→                continue
   237→            unique.append(name)
   238→            seen.add(name)
   239→        return unique
   240→    except Exception:
   241→        return None
   242→
   243→
   244→# ── Dynamic model catalog cache ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   245→# Populated at startup (background) and on demand; keyed by provider name.
   246→# Allows /api/model and /api/model-catalog to return the full live model list
   247→# without a blocking API call on every request.
   248→
   249→_dynamic_model_cache: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
   250→_dynamic_model_cache_lock = threading.Lock()
   251→ 
   252→
   253→def _get_available_models(provider: str, current_model: Optional[str] = None) -> List[str]:
   254→    """Return the best-available model list for a provider.
   255→
   256→    Preference order: cached live list > static PROVIDER_MODELS fallback.
   257→    If *current_model* is provided and not already in the list it is prepended
   258→    so the active model is always visible in the selector.
   259→    """
   260→    cached = _dynamic_model_cache.get(provider)
   261→    models: List[str] = list(cached) if cached is not None else list(PROVIDER_MODELS.get(provider, []))
   262→    if current_model and current_model not in models:
   263→        models = [current_model] + models
   264→    return models
   265→
   266→
   267→def _refresh_dynamic_model_cache(provider: str) -> None:
   268→    """Fetch the live model list for *provider* and store it in the cache (blocking)."""
   269→    discovered = _catalog_discover_provider_models(provider)
   270→    if discovered:
   271→        with _dynamic_model_cache_lock:
   272→            _dynamic_model_cache[provider] = discovered
   273→
   274→
   275→def _maybe_refresh_dynamic_cache_in_background(provider: str) -> None:
   276→    """Kick off a background refresh for *provider* if not already cached."""
   277→    if provider not in _CATALOG_LIST_MODELS_CAPABLE:
   278→        return
   279→    if provider in _dynamic_model_cache:
   280→        return
   281→    t = threading.Thread(
   282→        target=_refresh_dynamic_model_cache,
   283→        args=(provider,),
   284→        daemon=True,
   285→        name=f"model-catalog-{provider}",
   286→    )
   287→    t.start()
   288→
   289→
   290→def _text_similarity(query: str, target: str) -> float:
   291→    """Simple word-overlap similarity score (0–1) for response library search."""
   292→    _STOP = {
   293→        'a', 'an', 'the', 'and', 'or', 'for', 'in', 'of', 'to', 'is',
   294→        'are', 'was', 'were', 'i', 'my', 'your', 'we', 'our', 'this',
   295→        'that', 'it', 'with', 'as', 'by', 'at', 'on', 'be', 'have',
   296→        'has', 'had', 'do', 'does', 'did', 'will', 'would', 'can',
   297→        'could', 'should', 'may', 'might', 'from', 'into', 'about',
   298→    }
   299→    def _tok(s: str) -> set:
   300→        return {w.lower() for w in re.findall(r'\w+', s) if w.lower() not in _STOP and len(w) > 2}
   301→    q_tok = _tok(query)
   302→    t_tok = _tok(target)
   303→    if not q_tok or not t_tok:
   304→        return 0.0
   305→    return len(q_tok & t_tok) / max(len(q_tok), len(t_tok))
   306→
   307→
   308→_SCREENING_FORMAT_GUIDANCE: dict = {
   309→    'direct':    ('Direct/Concise',    '150–200 words',
   310→                  'Be clear and direct. State the answer, give one concrete example, close concisely.'),
   311→    'star':      ('STAR',              '250–350 words',
   312→                  'Use the STAR framework: Situation, Task, Action, Result. 1–2 sentences each.'),
   313→    'technical': ('Technical Detail', '400–500 words',
   314→                  'Provide full technical depth: context, methodology, tools/technologies, outcomes with metrics.'),
   315→}
   316→
   317→
   318→def _web_app_build_objects(args, auth_manager):
   319→    """
   320→    Instantiate LLMClient, CVOrchestrator, and ConversationManager from CLI args.
   321→
   322→    Defined at module level so tests can patch ``CVOrchestrator`` and
   323→    ``ConversationManager`` in ``scripts.web_app`` and have the patches
   324→    take effect when ``session_registry.create()`` calls this factory.
   325→    """
   326→    llm_client = get_llm_provider(
   327→        provider=args.llm_provider,
   328→        model=args.model,
   329→        auth_manager=auth_manager,
   330→    )
   331→    orchestrator = CVOrchestrator(
   332→        master_data_path=args.master_data,
   333→        publications_path=args.publications,
   334→        output_dir=args.output_dir,
   335→        llm_client=llm_client,
   336→    )
   337→    conversation = ConversationManager(orchestrator=orchestrator, llm_client=llm_client)
   338→    return conversation, orchestrator
   339→
   340→
   341→def create_app(args) -> Flask:
   342→    app = Flask(__name__, static_folder=None)
   343→
   344→    # Validate configuration before initializing dependencies.
   345→    # Raises ConfigurationError with a clear message if no LLM provider is set.
   346→    validate_config(provider=args.llm_provider)
   347→
   348→    # Kick off a background pricing-cache refresh if the cache is stale
   349→    maybe_refresh_in_background()
   350→
```

```text
   358→    # ── Provider / model state ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   359→    _provider_name: str = args.llm_provider
   360→    _current_model: Optional[str] = args.model  # short form; updated by set_model()

   365→        provider=_provider_name, model=args.model, auth_manager=auth_manager
   366→    )
```
