   137→
   138→
   139→@dataclass
   140→class MessageResponse:
   141→    """Response shape for POST /api/message."""
   142→    ok: bool
   143→    response: Any
   144→    phase: Optional[str]
   145→
   146→
   147→@dataclass
   148→class ActionResponse:
   149→    """Response shape for POST /api/action."""
   150→    ok: bool
   151→    result: Any
   152→    phase: Optional[str]
   153→
   154→
   155→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   156→# Model-catalog helpers (module-level for testability)
   157→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   158→
   159→_CATALOG_LIST_MODELS_CAPABLE: set[str] = {"openai", "anthropic", "gemini", "groq"}
   160→_CATALOG_STATIC_ONLY: set[str] = {"copilot-oauth", "copilot", "github", "local"}
   161→
   162→
   163→def _catalog_provider_api_key(provider: str) -> Optional[str]:
   164→    if provider == "openai":
   165→        return os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
   166→    if provider == "anthropic":
   167→        return os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
   168→    if provider == "gemini":
   169→        return os.getenv("GEMINI_API_KEY") or os.getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY")
   170→    if provider == "groq":
   171→        return os.getenv("GROQ_API_KEY")
   172→    return None
   173→
   174→
   175→def _catalog_provider_api_base(provider: str) -> Optional[str]:
   176→    if provider == "openai":
   177→        return os.getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL")
   178→    if provider == "anthropic":
   179→        return os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL")
   180→    if provider == "gemini":
   181→        return os.getenv("GOOGLE_GEMINI_BASE_URL")
   182→    if provider == "groq":
   183→        return os.getenv("GROQ_BASE_URL")
   184→    return None
   185→
   186→
   187→def _catalog_normalize_model_id(model_id: str) -> str:
   188→    """Normalize Gemini-style 'models/gemini-2.5-flash' IDs to bare model names."""
   189→    if model_id.startswith("models/"):
   190→        return model_id.split("/", 1)[1]
   191→    return model_id
   192→
   193→
   194→def _catalog_discover_provider_models(provider: str) -> Optional[List[str]]:
   195→    """Return runtime model list for providers that support list_models, or None."""
   196→    if provider not in _CATALOG_LIST_MODELS_CAPABLE or provider in _CATALOG_STATIC_ONLY:
   197→        return None
   198→
   199→    api_key = _catalog_provider_api_key(provider)
   200→    if not api_key:
   201→        return None
   202→
   203→    try:
   204→        from any_llm import list_models as anyllm_list_models
   205→    except Exception:
   206→        return None
   207→
   208→    try:
   209→        kwargs: Dict[str, Any] = {
   210→            "provider": provider,
   211→            "api_key":  api_key,
   212→        }
   213→        api_base = _catalog_provider_api_base(provider)
   214→        if api_base:
   215→            kwargs["api_base"] = api_base
   216→
   217→        models = anyllm_list_models(**kwargs)
   218→        names: List[str] = []
   219→        for item in models:
   220→            model_id = getattr(item, "id", None)
   221→            if model_id is None:
   222→                model_id = str(item)
   223→            model_id = _catalog_normalize_model_id(str(model_id))
   224→            if model_id:
   225→                names.append(model_id)
   226→
   227→        # Keep order stable and deduplicate.
   228→        unique: List[str] = []
   229→        seen: set[str] = set()
   230→        for name in names:
   231→            if name in seen:
   232→                continue
   233→            unique.append(name)
   234→            seen.add(name)
   235→        return unique
   236→    except Exception:
   237→        return None
   238→
   239→
   240→# ── Dynamic model catalog cache ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   241→# Populated at startup (background) and on demand; keyed by provider name.
   242→# Allows /api/model and /api/model-catalog to return the full live model list
   243→# without a blocking API call on every request.
   244→
   245→_dynamic_model_cache: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
   246→_dynamic_model_cache_lock = threading.Lock()
   247→ 
   248→
   249→def _get_available_models(provider: str, current_model: Optional[str] = None) -> List[str]:
   250→    """Return the best-available model list for a provider.
   251→
   252→    Preference order: cached live list > static PROVIDER_MODELS fallback.
   253→    If *current_model* is provided and not already in the list it is prepended
   254→    so the active model is always visible in the selector.
   255→    """
   256→    cached = _dynamic_model_cache.get(provider)
   257→    models: List[str] = list(cached) if cached is not None else list(PROVIDER_MODELS.get(provider, []))
   258→    if current_model and current_model not in models:
   259→        models = [current_model] + models
   260→    return models
   261→
   262→
   263→def _refresh_dynamic_model_cache(provider: str) -> None:
   264→    """Fetch the live model list for *provider* and store it in the cache (blocking)."""
   265→    discovered = _catalog_discover_provider_models(provider)
   266→    if discovered:
   267→        with _dynamic_model_cache_lock:
   268→            _dynamic_model_cache[provider] = discovered
   269→
   270→
   271→def _maybe_refresh_dynamic_cache_in_background(provider: str) -> None:
   272→    """Kick off a background refresh for *provider* if not already cached."""
   273→    if provider not in _CATALOG_LIST_MODELS_CAPABLE:
   274→        return
   275→    if provider in _dynamic_model_cache:
   276→        return
   277→    t = threading.Thread(
   278→        target=_refresh_dynamic_model_cache,
   279→        args=(provider,),
   280→        daemon=True,
   281→        name=f"model-catalog-{provider}",
   282→    )
   283→    t.start()
   284→
   285→
   286→def _text_similarity(query: str, target: str) -> float:
   287→    """Simple word-overlap similarity score (0–1) for response library search."""
   288→    _STOP = {
   289→        'a', 'an', 'the', 'and', 'or', 'for', 'in', 'of', 'to', 'is',
   290→        'are', 'was', 'were', 'i', 'my', 'your', 'we', 'our', 'this',
   291→        'that', 'it', 'with', 'as', 'by', 'at', 'on', 'be', 'have',
   292→        'has', 'had', 'do', 'does', 'did', 'will', 'would', 'can',
   293→        'could', 'should', 'may', 'might', 'from', 'into', 'about',
   294→    }
   295→    def _tok(s: str) -> set:
   296→        return {w.lower() for w in re.findall(r'\w+', s) if w.lower() not in _STOP and len(w) > 2}
   297→    q_tok = _tok(query)
   298→    t_tok = _tok(target)
   299→    if not q_tok or not t_tok:
   300→        return 0.0
   301→    return len(q_tok & t_tok) / max(len(q_tok), len(t_tok))
   302→
   303→
   304→_SCREENING_FORMAT_GUIDANCE: dict = {
   305→    'direct':    ('Direct/Concise',    '150–200 words',
   306→                  'Be clear and direct. State the answer, give one concrete example, close concisely.'),
   307→    'star':      ('STAR',              '250–350 words',
   308→                  'Use the STAR framework: Situation, Task, Action, Result. 1–2 sentences each.'),
   309→    'technical': ('Technical Detail', '400–500 words',
   310→                  'Provide full technical depth: context, methodology, tools/technologies, outcomes with metrics.'),
   311→}
   312→
   313→
   314→def _web_app_build_objects(args, auth_manager):
   315→    """
   316→    Instantiate LLMClient, CVOrchestrator, and ConversationManager from CLI args.
   317→
   318→    Defined at module level so tests can patch ``CVOrchestrator`` and
   319→    ``ConversationManager`` in ``scripts.web_app`` and have the patches
   320→    take effect when ``session_registry.create()`` calls this factory.
   321→    """
   322→    llm_client = get_llm_provider(
   323→        provider=args.llm_provider,
   324→        model=args.model,
   325→        auth_manager=auth_manager,
   326→    )
   327→    orchestrator = CVOrchestrator(
   328→        master_data_path=args.master_data,
   329→        publications_path=args.publications,
   330→        output_dir=args.output_dir,
   331→        llm_client=llm_client,
   332→    )
   333→    conversation = ConversationManager(orchestrator=orchestrator, llm_client=llm_client)
   334→    return conversation, orchestrator
   335→
   336→
   337→def create_app(args) -> Flask:
   338→    app = Flask(__name__, static_folder=None)
   339→
   340→    # Validate configuration before initializing dependencies.
   341→    # Raises ConfigurationError with a clear message if no LLM provider is set.
   342→    validate_config(provider=args.llm_provider)
   343→
   344→    # Kick off a background pricing-cache refresh if the cache is stale
   345→    maybe_refresh_in_background()
   346→
   347→    # Kick off a background dynamic model catalog refresh for the active provider
   348→    _maybe_refresh_dynamic_cache_in_background(args.llm_provider)
   349→
   350→    # Copilot OAuth auth manager (shared across all requests)
   351→    auth_manager = CopilotAuthManager()
   352→    _auth_poll: dict = {"polling": False, "error": None, "device_code": None, "interval": 5}
   353→
   354→    # ── Provider / model state ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   355→    _provider_name: str = args.llm_provider
   356→    _current_model: Optional[str] = args.model  # short form; updated by set_model()
   357→
   358→    # One shared LLM client used by model-catalog / test endpoints.
   359→    # Per-session LLM clients are created inside each SessionEntry.
   360→    llm_client = get_llm_provider(
   361→        provider=_provider_name, model=args.model, auth_manager=auth_manager
   362→    )
   363→
   364→    # ── Session registry ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   365→    # Replaces the single global conversation + orchestrator pair.
   366→    # Each browser tab gets its own session identified by a short UUID.
   367→    _app_config = get_config()
   368→
   369→    def _build_objects_for_registry(_config_ignored):
   370→        """Factory passed to SessionRegistry; uses the app's CLI args."""
   371→        return _web_app_build_objects(args, auth_manager)
   372→
   373→    session_registry = SessionRegistry(
   374→        idle_timeout_minutes=_app_config.idle_timeout_minutes,
   375→        build_objects=_build_objects_for_registry,
   376→    )
   377→    app.session_registry = session_registry  # exposed for test access
   378→
   379→    @app.before_request
   380→    def _evict_idle_sessions():
   381→        """Lazily evict stale sessions on every request."""
   382→        session_registry.evict_idle()
   383→
   384→    # ── Session helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   385→
   386→    def _get_session(required: bool = True):
   387→        """Extract session_id from the request and return the SessionEntry.
   388→
   389→        For GET requests reads from query string.
   390→        For POST/PUT/DELETE reads from query string OR JSON body.
   391→        Returns None (does not raise) when required=False and session_id absent.
   392→        Aborts 400 when required=True and session_id absent.
   393→        Aborts 404 when session_id present but not in registry.
   394→        """
   395→        from flask import abort as _abort
   396→        sid = request.args.get('session_id')
   397→        if not sid and request.is_json:
   398→            sid = (request.get_json(silent=True) or {}).get('session_id')
   399→        if not sid:
   400→            if required:
```

   407→
   408→    def _validate_owner(entry) -> None:
   409→        """Validate that the request's owner_token matches the session's owner.
   410→
   411→        Reads owner_token from the JSON body or query string (GET requests).
   412→        Aborts 403 if the token does not match.
   413→        Skips validation if the session has no owner set yet (unclaimed).
   414→        """
   415→        from flask import abort as _abort
   416→        if entry.owner_token is None:
   417→            return  # unclaimed — allow any caller
   418→        token = (request.get_json(silent=True) or {}).get('owner_token')
   419→        if token is None:
   420→            token = request.args.get('owner_token')
   421→        if token != entry.owner_token:
   422→            _abort(403, description='Not the session owner')
   423→
   424→    def _infer_position_name(job_text: str) -> Optional[str]:
   425→        """Infer a concise position label from job text."""
   426→        if not job_text:
   427→            return None
   428→
   429→        lines = [line.strip() for line in job_text.splitlines() if line.strip()]
   430→        if not lines:
   431→            return None
   432→
   433→        title = lines[0]
   434→        company = lines[1] if len(lines) > 1 else ""
   435→
   436→        if " at " in title.lower() and not company:
   437→            parts = title.split(" at ", 1)
   438→            if len(parts) == 2:
   439→                title, company = parts[0].strip(), parts[1].strip()
   440→
   441→        if title and company:
   442→            label = f"{title} at {company}"
   443→        else:
   444→            label = title or company
   445→
   446→        return label[:120] if label else None
   447→
   448→    def _coerce_to_dict(value: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   449→        """Return value as a dict, parsing from JSON string if necessary.
   450→
   451→        Defensive helper for session state fields that older sessions may have
   452→        persisted as a JSON string rather than a nested object.
   453→        """
   454→        if isinstance(value, dict):
   455→            return value
   456→        if isinstance(value, str):
   457→            try:
   458→                parsed = json.loads(value)
   459→                if isinstance(parsed, dict):
   460→                    return parsed
   461→            except json.JSONDecodeError:
   462→                pass
   463→        return {}
   464→
   465→    def _extract_json_payload(text: str) -> Any:
   466→        """Best-effort extraction of JSON array/object from model output.
   467→
   468→        Uses bracket-depth counting to locate the outermost JSON container
   469→        rather than greedy regexes, so it handles multiple code blocks,
   470→        backticks inside strings, and JSON embedded in prose correctly.
   471→        """
   472→        if not text:
   473→            return None
   474→
   475→        # Fast path: already clean JSON.
   476→        try:
   477→            return json.loads(text)
   478→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   479→            pass
   480→
   481→        # Find the first JSON container character.
   482→        start = -1
   483→        open_char = ''
   484→        for i, ch in enumerate(text):
   485→            if ch in ('{', '['):
   486→                start = i
   487→                open_char = ch
   488→                break
   489→        if start == -1:
   490→            return None
   491→
   492→        close_char = '}' if open_char == '{' else ']'
   493→        depth = 0
   494→        in_string = False
   495→        escape_next = False
   496→        for j in range(start, len(text)):
   497→            ch = text[j]
   498→            if escape_next:
   499→                escape_next = False
   500→                continue
   501→            if ch == '\\' and in_string:
   502→                escape_next = True
   503→                continue
   504→            if ch == '"':
   505→                in_string = not in_string
   506→                continue
   507→            if in_string:
   508→                continue
   509→            if ch == open_char:
   510→                depth += 1
   511→            elif ch == close_char:
   512→                depth -= 1
   513→                if depth == 0:
   514→                    try:
   515→                        return json.loads(text[start:j + 1])
   516→                    except json.JSONDecodeError:
   517→                        return None
   518→        return None
   519→
   520→    def _fallback_post_analysis_questions(analysis: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
   521→        """Deterministic fallback if LLM question generation fails."""
   522→        questions: List[Dict] = []
   523→
   524→        role_level = analysis.get("role_level")
   525→        if role_level:
   526→            questions.append({
   527→                "type": "experience_level",
   528→                "question": f"This role appears to be at {role_level} level. Should I emphasize your most senior experiences or include a broader range to show career progression?",
   529→                "choices": ["Emphasize most senior", "Broader career progression", "Let you decide based on analysis"],
   530→            })
   531→
   532→        required_skills = analysis.get("required_skills") or []
   533→        if isinstance(required_skills, list):
   534→            skill_text = " ".join(str(s).lower() for s in required_skills)
   535→            if any(token in skill_text for token in ("leadership", "management", "team")):
   536→                questions.append({
   537→                    "type": "leadership_focus",
   538→                    "question": "This role has leadership components. Would you prefer me to emphasize your management experience or focus more on your technical contributions?",
   539→                    "choices": ["Emphasize management", "Focus on technical", "Balance both equally"],
   540→                })
   541→
   542→        domain = analysis.get("domain")
   543→        if domain:
   544→            questions.append({
   545→                "type": "domain_expertise",
   546→                "question": f"The role is in {domain}. Do you have particular projects or achievements in this domain that you'd like me to highlight?",
   547→                "choices": ["Highlight domain-specific achievements", "Use all available experience", "Prioritize most recent work"],
   548→            })
   549→
   550→        company = analysis.get("company")
   551→        if company:
   552→            questions.append({
   553→                "type": "company_culture",
   554→                "question": f"For {company}, would you like me to tailor emphasis toward their culture and values? If so, what should I prioritize?",
   555→                "choices": ["Research-driven / academic", "Industry / commercial impact", "Innovation / startup", "Use cultural indicators from job description"],
   556→            })
   557→
   558→        return questions[:4]
   559→
   560→    def _generate_post_analysis_questions(
   561→        analysis: Dict[str, Any],
   562→        job_text: Optional[str],
   563→        prior_qa: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   564→    ) -> List[Dict]:
   565→        """Generate clarifying questions from the LLM in JSON format.
   566→
   567→        ``prior_qa`` is a dict mapping previous question keys to the candidate's
   568→        answers; when supplied the LLM is instructed to avoid redundant questions
   569→        and focus only on gaps not yet covered.
   570→        """
   571→        prior_section = ""
   572→        if prior_qa:
   573→            lines = "\n".join(f"- {k}: {v}" for k, v in prior_qa.items())
   574→            prior_section = f"""
   575→Previously answered questions (do NOT repeat these topics):
   576→{lines}
   577→
   578→"""
   579→        prompt = f"""You are helping tailor a CV to a specific job.
   580→
   581→Create 2-4 concise, high-value clarifying questions for the candidate before generating customization recommendations.
   582→
   583→Requirements:
   584→- Questions must be specific to this role, company, and analysis.
   585→- Focus on tradeoffs that affect selection/emphasis of experiences and skills.
   586→- Avoid generic or repetitive questions.
   587→- Keep each question under 220 characters.
   588→- For each question, provide 2-4 button-answer choices covering the most likely responses.
   589→- Return ONLY valid JSON as an array of objects.
   590→{prior_section}
   591→Schema:
   592→[
   593→  {{"type": "short_snake_case", "question": "...", "choices": ["Option A", "Option B", "Option C"]}}
   594→]
   595→
   596→Job Analysis:
   597→{json.dumps(analysis, indent=2)}
   598→
   599→Job Description (excerpt):
   600→{(job_text or '')[:2500]}
   601→"""
   602→
   603→        response = llm_client.chat(
   604→            messages=[
   605→                {"role": "system", "content": "You generate targeted CV-optimization clarification questions and respond with strict JSON."},
   606→                {"role": "user", "content": prompt},
   607→            ],
   608→            temperature=0.4,
   609→        )
   610→
   611→        payload = _extract_json_payload(response)
   612→        if isinstance(payload, dict) and isinstance(payload.get("questions"), list):
   613→            payload = payload.get("questions")
   614→
   615→        if not isinstance(payload, list):
   616→            return []
   617→
   618→        cleaned: List[Dict] = []
   619→        for item in payload:
   620→            if not isinstance(item, dict):
   621→                continue
   622→            question = str(item.get("question", "")).strip()
   623→            qtype = str(item.get("type", "clarification")).strip().lower().replace(" ", "_")
   624→            choices = item.get("choices")
   625→            if not isinstance(choices, list):
   626→                choices = []
   627→            choices = [str(c).strip() for c in choices if str(c).strip()][:4]
   628→            if not question:
   629→                continue
   630→            entry: Dict = {
   631→                "type": qtype[:40] or "clarification",
   632→                "question": question[:220],
   633→            }
   634→            if choices:
   635→                entry["choices"] = choices
   636→            cleaned.append(entry)
   637→
   638→        return cleaned[:4]
   639→
   640→    # Preload job description if provided — create a session for it
   641→    _preload_session_id: Optional[str] = None
   642→    if args.job_file:
   643→        job_file_path = Path(args.job_file)
   644→        if job_file_path.exists():
   645→            job_text = job_file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   646→            _preload_sid, _preload_entry = session_registry.create(_app_config)
   647→            _preload_session_id = _preload_sid
   648→            _preload_conv = _preload_entry.manager
   649→            _preload_conv.add_job_description(job_text)
   650→            # Extract position name from filename (remove date suffix and extension)
   651→            position_name = job_file_path.stem
   652→            position_name = re.sub(r'_\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$', '', position_name)
   653→            _preload_conv.state["position_name"] = position_name
   654→            print(f"✓ Position name set to: {position_name}")
   655→            print(f"✓ Pre-loaded session ID: {_preload_sid}")
   656→
   657→            # Try to load the most recent session for this position
   658→            try:
   659→                loaded = _preload_conv._load_latest_session_for_position(position_name)
   660→                if loaded:
   661→                    print(f"✓ Restored previous session for: {position_name}")
   662→                else:
   663→                    _preload_conv.conversation_history.append({
   664→                        "role": "system",
   665→                        "content": (
   666→                            f"Job description loaded: {job_text.split(chr(10))[0]} at "
   667→                            + (job_text.split(chr(10))[1] if len(job_text.split(chr(10))) > 1 else 'Company')
   668→                        ),
   669→                    })
   670→            except Exception as e:
   671→                print(f"⚠ Could not load previous session: {e}")
   672→                _preload_conv.conversation_history.append({
   673→                    "role": "system",
   674→                    "content": (
   675→                        f"Job description loaded: {job_text.split(chr(10))[0]} at "
   676→                        + (job_text.split(chr(10))[1] if len(job_text.split(chr(10))) > 1 else 'Company')
   677→                    ),
   678→                })
   679→            session_registry.touch(_preload_sid)
   680→
   681→    @app.get("/")
   682→    def index():
   683→        if _preload_session_id and not request.args.get("session"):
   684→            return redirect(url_for("index", session=_preload_session_id))
   685→        page_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "web" / "index.html"
   686→        return send_file(page_path)
   687→
   688→    @app.get("/favicon.ico")
   689→    def favicon():
   690→        return "", 204
   691→
   692→    @app.get("/<path:filename>")
   693→    def static_web(filename):
   694→        web_dir = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "web"
   695→        return send_from_directory(web_dir, filename)
   696→
   697→    @app.get("/logo")
   698→    def logo():
   699→        # Serve white on transparent logo from web/media
   700→        logo_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "web" / "media" / "logo_white_transparent.png"
   701→        if logo_path.exists():
   702→            return send_file(logo_path)
   703→        else:
   704→            # Return 404 if logo not found
   705→            return "", 404
   706→
   707→    @app.get("/api/status")
   708→    def status():
   709→        # Read-only: no lock, no touch.
   710→        # When no session_id is present return a minimal liveness response so
   711→        # health-check probes (test harness, conftest) get HTTP 200 without
   712→        # needing an active session.
   713→        entry = _get_session(required=False)
   714→        if entry is None:
   715→            return jsonify({
   716→                "ok": True,
   717→                "alive": True,
   718→                "phase": None,
   719→                "llm_provider": _provider_name,
   720→                "llm_model": _current_model,
   721→            })
   722→        conversation = entry.manager
   723→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
   724→        # Get all experience IDs from master data
   725→        all_experience_ids = []
   726→        all_skills = []
   727→        all_achievements = []
   728→        professional_summaries = {}
   729→        if orchestrator and orchestrator.master_data:
   730→            experiences = orchestrator.master_data.get('experience', [])
   731→            all_experience_ids = [exp.get('id') for exp in experiences if exp.get('id')]
   732→            all_achievements = orchestrator.master_data.get('selected_achievements', [])
   733→            professional_summaries = dict(orchestrator.master_data.get('professional_summaries', {}))
   734→            # Merge in any LLM-generated summaries saved this session (e.g. 'ai_recommended')
   735→            session_summaries = conversation.state.get('session_summaries') or {}
   736→            professional_summaries.update(session_summaries)
   737→
   738→            # Get all skills - use canonical flat list format
   739→            skills_data = orchestrator.master_data.get('skills', [])
   740→            all_skills = conversation.normalize_skills_data(skills_data)
   741→        return jsonify(dataclasses.asdict(StatusResponse(
   742→            position_name=conversation.state.get("position_name"),
   743→            phase=conversation.state.get("phase"),
   744→            llm_provider=_provider_name,
   745→            llm_model=_current_model,
   746→            job_description=bool(conversation.state.get("job_description")),
   747→            job_description_text=conversation.state.get("job_description"),
   748→            job_analysis=conversation.state.get("job_analysis"),  # full data, not just bool
   749→            post_analysis_questions=conversation.state.get("post_analysis_questions") or [],
   750→            post_analysis_answers=conversation.state.get("post_analysis_answers") or {},
   751→            customizations=conversation.state.get("customizations"),  # full data, not just bool
   752→            generated_files=conversation.state.get("generated_files"),
   753→            generation_progress=conversation.state.get("generation_progress") or [],
   754→            persuasion_warnings=conversation.state.get("persuasion_warnings") or [],
   755→            all_experience_ids=all_experience_ids,
   756→            all_skills=all_skills,
   757→            all_achievements=all_achievements,
   758→            professional_summaries=professional_summaries,
   759→            copilot_auth=auth_manager.status,
   760→            iterating=bool(conversation.state.get("iterating")),
   761→            reentry_phase=conversation.state.get("reentry_phase"),
   762→            experience_decisions=conversation.state.get("experience_decisions")   or {},
   763→            skill_decisions=conversation.state.get("skill_decisions")         or {},
   764→            achievement_decisions=conversation.state.get("achievement_decisions")   or {},
   765→            publication_decisions=conversation.state.get("publication_decisions")   or {},
   766→            summary_focus_override=conversation.state.get("summary_focus_override"),
   767→            extra_skills=conversation.state.get("extra_skills")            or [],
   768→            session_file=str(getattr(conversation, "session_file", "") or ""),
   769→            max_skills=int(conversation.state.get("max_skills") or get_config().get("generation.max_skills", 20)),
   770→            achievement_edits=conversation.state.get("achievement_edits")       or {},
   771→            intake=conversation.state.get("intake")                             or {},
   772→        )))
   773→
   774→    @app.get("/api/master-fields")
   775→    def master_fields():
   776→        """Return selected_achievements and professional_summaries directly from the master CV file.
   777→
   778→        This endpoint re-reads directly from disk to bypass any in-memory state
   779→        issues, ensuring the data is always fresh and available.
   780→        """
   781→        entry = _get_session()
   782→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
   783→        try:
   784→            with open(orchestrator.master_data_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
   785→                data = json.load(f)
   786→            return jsonify({
   787→                "ok": True,
   788→                "selected_achievements":   data.get('selected_achievements', []),
   789→                "professional_summaries":  data.get('professional_summaries', {}),
   790→                "experiences":             data.get('experience', []),
   791→            })
   792→        except Exception as e:
   793→            return jsonify({
   794→                "ok": False,
   795→                "error": str(e),
   796→                "selected_achievements":  [],
   797→                "professional_summaries": {},
   798→                "experiences":            [],
   799→            }), 500
```

   835→    @app.post("/api/master-data/update-achievement")
   836→    def master_data_update_achievement():
   837→        """Update or delete a selected achievement, or add a new one to the master CV.
   838→
   839→        Body:
   840→            id     — achievement id (required)
   841→            action — 'delete' removes the entry; omit or any other value upserts
   842→        """
   843→        entry = _get_session()
   844→        _validate_owner(entry)
   845→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
   846→        req    = request.get_json() or {}
   847→        ach_id = (req.get('id') or '').strip()
   848→        if not ach_id:
   849→            return jsonify({"error": "id is required"}), 400
   850→        try:
   851→            master, master_path = _load_master(orchestrator.master_data_path)
   852→            achievements = master.setdefault('selected_achievements', [])
   853→            if req.get('action') == 'delete':
   854→                original_len = len(achievements)
   855→                master['selected_achievements'] = [a for a in achievements if a.get('id') != ach_id]
   856→                if len(master['selected_achievements']) == original_len:
   857→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Achievement not found"}), 404
   858→                _save_master(master, master_path)
   859→                return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "deleted", "id": ach_id})
   860→            existing = next((a for a in achievements if a.get('id') == ach_id), None)
   861→            if existing:
   862→                for field in ('title', 'description', 'relevant_for', 'importance'):
   863→                    if field in req:
   864→                        existing[field] = req[field]
   865→                action = 'updated'
   866→            else:
   867→                new_ach = {'id': ach_id}
   868→                for field in ('title', 'description', 'relevant_for', 'importance'):
   869→                    if field in req:
   870→                        new_ach[field] = req[field]
   871→                achievements.append(new_ach)
   872→                action = 'added'
   873→            _save_master(master, master_path)
   874→            return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": action, "id": ach_id})
   875→        except Exception as e:
   876→            return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": str(e)}), 500
   877→
   878→    @app.post("/api/master-data/update-summary")
   879→    def master_data_update_summary():
   880→        """Update, add, or delete a named professional summary variant in the master CV.
   881→
   882→        Body:
   883→            key    — variant name/slug (required)
   884→            text   — summary text (required unless action is 'delete')
   885→            action — 'delete' removes the variant; omit or any other value upserts
   886→        """
   887→        entry = _get_session()
   888→        _validate_owner(entry)
   889→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
   890→        req  = request.get_json() or {}
   891→        key  = (req.get('key') or '').strip()
   892→        text = (req.get('text') or '').strip()
   893→        if not key:
   894→            return jsonify({"error": "key is required"}), 400
   895→        action = req.get('action')
   896→        if action != 'delete' and not text:
   897→            return jsonify({"error": "text is required for add/update"}), 400
   898→        try:
   899→            master, master_path = _load_master(orchestrator.master_data_path)
   900→            summaries = master.get('professional_summaries', {})
   901→            if isinstance(summaries, list):
   902→                summaries = {str(i): v for i, v in enumerate(summaries)}
   903→                master['professional_summaries'] = summaries
   904→            if action == 'delete':
   905→                if key not in summaries:
   906→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Summary not found"}), 404
   907→                del summaries[key]
   908→                master['professional_summaries'] = summaries
   909→                _save_master(master, master_path)
   910→                return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "deleted", "key": key})
   911→            is_new = key not in summaries
   912→            summaries[key] = text
   913→            master['professional_summaries'] = summaries
   914→            _save_master(master, master_path)
   915→            return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "added" if is_new else "updated", "key": key})
   916→        except Exception as e:
   917→            return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": str(e)}), 500
   918→
   919→    @app.get("/api/master-data/full")
   920→    def master_data_full():
   921→        """Return all editable sections of the master CV for the structured editor."""
   922→        entry = _get_session()
   923→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
   924→        try:
   925→            master, _ = _load_master(orchestrator.master_data_path)
   926→            return jsonify({
   927→                "ok":                     True,
   928→                "personal_info":          master.get('personal_info', {}),
   929→                "experience":             master.get('experience', master.get('experiences', [])),
   930→                "skills":                 master.get('skills', []),
   931→                "education":              master.get('education', []),
   932→                "awards":                 master.get('awards', []),
   933→                "selected_achievements":  master.get('selected_achievements', []),
   934→                "professional_summaries": master.get('professional_summaries', {}),
   935→            })
   936→        except Exception as e:
   937→            return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": str(e)}), 500
   938→
   939→    @app.post("/api/master-data/personal-info")
   940→    def master_data_update_personal_info():
   941→        """Update personal_info fields in the master CV.
   942→
   943→        Body (all optional — only provided keys are updated):
   944→            name, title, email, phone, linkedin, website, city, state
   945→        """
   946→        entry = _get_session()
   947→        _validate_owner(entry)
   948→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
   949→        req = request.get_json() or {}
   950→        try:
   951→            master, master_path = _load_master(orchestrator.master_data_path)
   952→            pi = master.setdefault('personal_info', {})
   953→            for field in ('name', 'title'):
   954→                if field in req:
   955→                    pi[field] = req[field]
   956→            contact = pi.setdefault('contact', {})
   957→            for field in ('email', 'phone', 'linkedin', 'website'):
   958→                if field in req:
   959→                    contact[field] = req[field]
   960→            if 'city' in req or 'state' in req:
   961→                address = contact.setdefault('address', {})
   962→                if 'city' in req:
   963→                    address['city'] = req['city']
   964→                if 'state' in req:
   965→                    address['state'] = req['state']
   966→            _save_master(master, master_path)
   967→            return jsonify({"ok": True})
   968→        except Exception as e:
   969→            return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": str(e)}), 500
   970→
   971→    @app.post("/api/master-data/experience")
   972→    def master_data_update_experience():
   973→        """Add, update, or delete an experience entry in the master CV.
   974→
   975→        Body:
   976→            action     — 'add' | 'update' | 'delete'
   977→            id         — experience id (required for update/delete)
   978→            experience — dict with fields: title, company, city, state,
   979→                         start_date, end_date, employment_type, importance,
   980→                         tags, domain_relevance
   981→        """
   982→        entry = _get_session()
   983→        _validate_owner(entry)
   984→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
   985→        req    = request.get_json() or {}
   986→        action = (req.get('action') or '').strip()
   987→        if action not in ('add', 'update', 'delete'):
   988→            return jsonify({"error": "action must be add, update, or delete"}), 400
   989→        try:
   990→            master, master_path = _load_master(orchestrator.master_data_path)
   991→            experiences = master.get('experience', master.pop('experiences', []))
   992→            master['experience'] = experiences
   993→            if action == 'delete':
   994→                exp_id = (req.get('id') or '').strip()
   995→                if not exp_id:
   996→                    return jsonify({"error": "id is required for delete"}), 400
   997→                original_len = len(experiences)
   998→                master['experience'] = [e for e in experiences if e.get('id') != exp_id]
   999→                if len(master['experience']) == original_len:
  1000→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Experience not found"}), 404
  1001→                _save_master(master, master_path)
  1002→                return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "deleted"})
  1003→            exp_data = req.get('experience') or {}
  1004→            if not exp_data.get('title') or not exp_data.get('company'):
  1005→                return jsonify({"error": "title and company are required"}), 400
  1006→            if action == 'add':
  1007→                new_id  = 'exp_' + str(int(datetime.now().timestamp() * 1000))
  1008→                loc: Dict[str, Any] = {}
  1009→                if exp_data.get('city'):
  1010→                    loc['city'] = exp_data['city']
  1011→                if exp_data.get('state'):
  1012→                    loc['state'] = exp_data['state']
  1013→                new_exp: Dict[str, Any] = {
  1014→                    'id':               new_id,
  1015→                    'title':            exp_data['title'],
  1016→                    'company':          exp_data['company'],
  1017→                    'location':         loc,
  1018→                    'start_date':       exp_data.get('start_date', ''),
  1019→                    'end_date':         exp_data.get('end_date', ''),
  1020→                    'employment_type':  exp_data.get('employment_type', 'full_time'),
  1021→                    'importance':       int(exp_data.get('importance') or 5),
  1022→                    'tags':             exp_data.get('tags') or [],
  1023→                    'domain_relevance': exp_data.get('domain_relevance') or [],
  1024→                    'achievements':     [],
  1025→                }
  1026→                experiences.append(new_exp)
  1027→                _save_master(master, master_path)
  1028→                return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "added", "id": new_id})
  1029→            exp_id = (req.get('id') or exp_data.get('id') or '').strip()
  1030→            if not exp_id:
  1031→                return jsonify({"error": "id is required for update"}), 400
  1032→            existing_exp = next((e for e in experiences if e.get('id') == exp_id), None)
  1033→            if not existing_exp:
  1034→                return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Experience not found"}), 404
  1035→            for field in ('title', 'company', 'start_date', 'end_date', 'employment_type'):
  1036→                if field in exp_data:
  1037→                    existing_exp[field] = exp_data[field]
  1038→            if 'importance' in exp_data:
  1039→                existing_exp['importance'] = int(exp_data['importance'])
  1040→            if 'city' in exp_data or 'state' in exp_data:
  1041→                exp_loc = existing_exp.setdefault('location', {})
  1042→                if 'city' in exp_data:
  1043→                    exp_loc['city'] = exp_data['city']
  1044→                if 'state' in exp_data:
  1045→                    exp_loc['state'] = exp_data['state']
  1046→            for field in ('tags', 'domain_relevance'):
  1047→                if field in exp_data:
  1048→                    existing_exp[field] = exp_data[field]
  1049→            _save_master(master, master_path)
  1050→            return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "updated", "id": exp_id})
  1051→        except Exception as e:
  1052→            return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": str(e)}), 500
  1053→
  1054→    @app.post("/api/master-data/skill")
  1055→    def master_data_update_skill():
  1056→        """Add or delete a skill, or manage skill categories in the master CV.
  1057→
  1058→        skill add/delete body:
  1059→            action   — 'add' | 'delete'
  1060→            skill    — skill name string
  1061→            category — category key (required when skills is a dict)
  1062→
  1063→        category management body:
  1064→            action        — 'add_category' | 'delete_category'
  1065→            category_key  — slug key
  1066→            category_name — display name (for add_category only)
  1067→        """
  1068→        entry = _get_session()
  1069→        _validate_owner(entry)
  1070→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
  1071→        req    = request.get_json() or {}
  1072→        action = (req.get('action') or '').strip()
  1073→        if action not in ('add', 'delete', 'add_category', 'delete_category'):
  1074→            return jsonify({"error": "action must be add, delete, add_category, or delete_category"}), 400
  1075→        try:
  1076→            master, master_path = _load_master(orchestrator.master_data_path)
  1077→            skills = master.get('skills', [])
  1078→            if action == 'add_category':
  1079→                cat_key  = (req.get('category_key') or '').strip()
  1080→                cat_name = (req.get('category_name') or cat_key).strip()
  1081→                if not cat_key:
  1082→                    return jsonify({"error": "category_key is required"}), 400
  1083→                if not isinstance(skills, dict):
  1084→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Skills field is not a category dict"}), 400
  1085→                if cat_key in skills:
  1086→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Category already exists"}), 409
  1087→                skills[cat_key] = {"category": cat_name, "skills": []}
  1088→                master['skills'] = skills
  1089→                _save_master(master, master_path)
  1090→                return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "category_added"})
  1091→            if action == 'delete_category':
  1092→                cat_key = (req.get('category_key') or '').strip()
  1093→                if not cat_key:
  1094→                    return jsonify({"error": "category_key is required"}), 400
  1095→                if not isinstance(skills, dict):
  1096→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Skills field is not a category dict"}), 400
  1097→                if cat_key not in skills:
  1098→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Category not found"}), 404
  1099→                del skills[cat_key]
  1100→                master['skills'] = skills
  1101→                _save_master(master, master_path)
  1102→                return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "category_deleted"})
  1103→            skill_name = (req.get('skill') or '').strip()
  1104→            if not skill_name:
  1105→                return jsonify({"error": "skill is required"}), 400
  1106→            if isinstance(skills, list):
  1107→                if action == 'add':
  1108→                    if skill_name in skills:
  1109→                        return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Skill already exists"}), 409
  1110→                    skills.append(skill_name)
  1111→                    master['skills'] = skills
  1112→                    _save_master(master, master_path)
  1113→                    return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "added"})
  1114→                if skill_name not in skills:
  1115→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Skill not found"}), 404
  1116→                skills.remove(skill_name)
  1117→                master['skills'] = skills
  1118→                _save_master(master, master_path)
  1119→                return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "deleted"})
  1120→            if isinstance(skills, dict):
  1121→                cat_key = (req.get('category') or '').strip()
  1122→                if not cat_key:
  1123→                    return jsonify({"error": "category is required for categorized skills"}), 400
  1124→                if cat_key not in skills:
  1125→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Category not found"}), 404
  1126→                cat_val  = skills[cat_key]
  1127→                cat_list: List[str] = (
  1128→                    cat_val if isinstance(cat_val, list)
  1129→                    else cat_val.setdefault('skills', []) if isinstance(cat_val, dict)
  1130→                    else []
  1131→                )
  1132→                if action == 'add':
  1133→                    if skill_name in cat_list:
  1134→                        return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Skill already exists in category"}), 409
  1135→                    cat_list.append(skill_name)
  1136→                    _save_master(master, master_path)
  1137→                    return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "added"})
  1138→                if skill_name not in cat_list:
  1139→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Skill not found in category"}), 404
  1140→                cat_list.remove(skill_name)
  1141→                _save_master(master, master_path)
  1142→                return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "deleted"})
  1143→            return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Unexpected skills format"}), 400
  1144→        except Exception as e:
  1145→            return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": str(e)}), 500
  1146→
  1147→    @app.post("/api/master-data/education")
  1148→    def master_data_update_education():
  1149→        """Add, update, or delete an education entry in the master CV.
  1150→
  1151→        Body:
  1152→            action      — 'add' | 'update' | 'delete'
  1153→            idx         — list index (int, required for update/delete)
  1154→            degree, field, institution, city, state, start_year, end_year
  1155→        """
  1156→        entry = _get_session()
  1157→        _validate_owner(entry)
  1158→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
  1159→        req    = request.get_json() or {}
  1160→        action = (req.get('action') or '').strip()
  1161→        if action not in ('add', 'update', 'delete'):
  1162→            return jsonify({"error": "action must be add, update, or delete"}), 400
  1163→        try:
  1164→            master, master_path = _load_master(orchestrator.master_data_path)
  1165→            education = master.setdefault('education', [])
  1166→            if action == 'delete':
  1167→                idx = req.get('idx')
  1168→                if not isinstance(idx, int):
  1169→                    return jsonify({"error": "idx (int) is required for delete"}), 400
  1170→                if idx < 0 or idx >= len(education):
  1171→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Index out of range"}), 404
  1172→                education.pop(idx)
  1173→                _save_master(master, master_path)
  1174→                return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "deleted"})
  1175→            edu_data: Dict[str, Any] = {}
  1176→            for field in ('degree', 'field', 'institution'):
  1177→                if field in req:
  1178→                    edu_data[field] = req[field]
  1179→            if req.get('city') or req.get('state'):
  1180→                loc: Dict[str, Any] = {}
  1181→                if req.get('city'):
  1182→                    loc['city'] = req['city']
  1183→                if req.get('state'):
  1184→                    loc['state'] = req['state']
  1185→                edu_data['location'] = loc
  1186→            for field in ('start_year', 'end_year'):
  1187→                if req.get(field) is not None:
  1188→                    edu_data[field] = int(req[field])
  1189→            if action == 'add':
  1190→                if not edu_data.get('degree') or not edu_data.get('institution'):
  1191→                    return jsonify({"error": "degree and institution are required"}), 400
  1192→                education.append(edu_data)
  1193→                _save_master(master, master_path)
  1194→                return jsonify({"ok": True, "action": "added", "idx": len(education) - 1})
  1195→            idx = req.get('idx')
  1196→            if not isinstance(idx, int):
  1197→                return jsonify({"error": "idx (int) is required for update"}), 400
  1198→            if idx < 0 or idx >= len(education):
  1199→                return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "Index out of range"}), 404
```

  1259→    @app.post("/api/generate-summary")
  1260→    def generate_professional_summary():
  1261→        """Generate (or refine) a custom LLM professional summary for this session.
  1262→
  1263→        Body (JSON, all optional):
  1264→            refinement_prompt: str   — user instructions for iterative refinement
  1265→            previous_summary:  str   — existing text to refine (required when
  1266→                                       refinement_prompt is provided)
  1267→
  1268→        On success stores the result as ``session_summaries['ai_generated']`` and
  1269→        auto-selects it via ``summary_focus_override``.
  1270→
  1271→        Returns:
  1272→            {"ok": true, "summary": "<text>"}
  1273→        """
  1274→        entry = _get_session()
  1275→        _validate_owner(entry)
  1276→        conversation = entry.manager
  1277→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
  1278→        sid = entry.session_id
  1279→        try:
  1280→            req = request.get_json() or {}
  1281→            refinement_prompt = (req.get('refinement_prompt') or '').strip() or None
  1282→            previous_summary  = (req.get('previous_summary')  or '').strip() or None
  1283→
  1284→            job_analysis = conversation.state.get('job_analysis')
  1285→            if not job_analysis:
  1286→                return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "No job analysis found. Analyse a job description first."}), 400
  1287→
  1288→            # Build a compact list of selected experiences.
  1289→            # Prefer the user's submitted decisions (experience_decisions) over the
  1290→            # LLM's original recommendations, so the summary reflects what the user
  1291→            # actually chose to include.
  1292→            all_experiences = orchestrator.master_data.get('experience', []) if orchestrator and orchestrator.master_data else []
  1293→            experience_decisions = conversation.state.get('experience_decisions') or {}
  1294→            if experience_decisions:
  1295→                # Include experiences the user accepted (emphasize or include)
  1296→                selected_exp_ids = {
  1297→                    eid for eid, action in experience_decisions.items()
  1298→                    if action in ('emphasize', 'include')
  1299→                }
  1300→                selected_experiences = [e for e in all_experiences if e.get('id') in selected_exp_ids]
  1301→            else:
  1302→                # Fall back to LLM recommendations if user hasn't submitted yet
  1303→                customizations = conversation.state.get('customizations') or {}
  1304→                recommended_ids = set(customizations.get('recommended_experiences') or [])
  1305→                selected_experiences = (
  1306→                    [e for e in all_experiences if e.get('id') in recommended_ids]
  1307→                    if recommended_ids else all_experiences
  1308→                )
  1309→
  1310→            with entry.lock:
  1311→                summary = llm_client.generate_professional_summary(
  1312→                    job_analysis=job_analysis,
  1313→                    master_data=orchestrator.master_data if orchestrator else {},
  1314→                    selected_experiences=selected_experiences,
  1315→                    refinement_prompt=refinement_prompt,
  1316→                    previous_summary=previous_summary,
  1317→                )
  1318→
  1319→                if not summary:
  1320→                    return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": "LLM returned an empty summary. Please try again."}), 500
  1321→
  1322→                # Persist in session_summaries so the orchestrator can resolve it
  1323→                session_summaries = conversation.state.get('session_summaries') or {}
  1324→                session_summaries['ai_generated'] = summary
  1325→                conversation.state['session_summaries'] = session_summaries
  1326→                # Auto-select the generated summary
  1327→                conversation.state['summary_focus_override'] = 'ai_generated'
  1328→                conversation._save_session()
  1329→            session_registry.touch(sid)
  1330→
  1331→            return jsonify({"ok": True, "summary": summary})
  1332→
  1333→        except Exception as exc:
  1334→            traceback.print_exc()
  1335→            return jsonify({"ok": False, "error": str(exc)}), 500

  1338→
  1339→    _TONE_GUIDANCE: Dict[str, str] = {
  1340→        'startup/tech':   'Energetic, direct, outcome-focused.  Emphasise velocity, impact, and technical depth.',
  1341→        'pharma/biotech': 'Precise, methodical, compliance-aware.  Reference domain credentials and regulatory rigour.',
  1342→        'academia':       'Scholarly, collaborative.  Highlight publications, teaching experience, and departmental service.',
  1343→        'financial':      'Professional, quantitative, risk-aware.  Emphasise fiduciary responsibility and data-driven decisions.',

  1345→    }

  1347→    @app.get("/api/cover-letter/prior")
  1348→    def cover_letter_prior():
  1349→        """Scan prior sessions for saved cover letters and return previews."""
  1350→        try:
  1351→            from utils.config import get_config
  1352→            cfg         = get_config()
  1353→            output_base = Path(cfg.get('data.output_dir', '~/CV/files')).expanduser()
  1354→            results     = []
  1355→            if output_base.exists():
  1356→                for session_file in sorted(output_base.rglob('session.json'), reverse=True)[:30]:
  1357→                    try:
  1358→                        with open(session_file, encoding='utf-8') as f:
  1359→                            data = json.load(f)
  1360→                        state = data.get('state', {})
  1361→                        cl_text = state.get('cover_letter_text')
  1362→                        if not cl_text:
  1363→                            continue
  1364→                        params = state.get('cover_letter_params') or {}
  1365→                        job_analysis = state.get('job_analysis') or {}
  1366→                        results.append({
  1367→                            'session_path': str(session_file),
  1368→                            'company':      job_analysis.get('company', ''),
  1369→                            'role':         job_analysis.get('title', ''),
  1370→                            'date':         data.get('timestamp', '')[:10],
  1371→                            'tone':         params.get('tone', ''),
  1372→                            'preview':      cl_text[:200],
  1373→                            'full_text':    cl_text,
  1374→                        })
  1375→                    except Exception:
  1376→                        pass
  1377→            return jsonify({'ok': True, 'sessions': results})
  1378→        except Exception as e:
  1379→            return jsonify({'ok': False, 'error': str(e)}), 500
  1380→
  1381→    @app.post("/api/cover-letter/generate")
  1382→    def cover_letter_generate():
  1383→        """Generate a cover letter using the LLM and current session context."""
  1384→        entry = _get_session()
  1385→        _validate_owner(entry)
  1386→        conversation = entry.manager
  1387→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
  1388→        sid = entry.session_id
  1389→        with entry.lock:
  1390→            body            = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
  1391→            tone            = body.get('tone', 'startup/tech')
  1392→            hiring_manager  = (body.get('hiring_manager') or 'Hiring Manager').strip()
  1393→            company_address = (body.get('company_address') or '').strip()
  1394→            highlight       = (body.get('highlight') or '').strip()
  1395→            reuse_body      = (body.get('reuse_body') or '').strip()
  1396→
  1397→            job_analysis  = conversation.state.get('job_analysis') or {}
  1398→            master        = orchestrator.master_data or {}
  1399→            personal_info = master.get('personal_info', {})
  1400→
  1401→            # Build skills/summary snippet from master data
  1402→            skills_raw = master.get('skills', [])
  1403→            all_skills = conversation.normalize_skills_data(skills_raw)
  1404→            skill_names = [
  1405→                s.get('name', str(s)) if isinstance(s, dict) else str(s)
  1406→                for s in all_skills
  1407→            ]
  1408→            top_skills = ', '.join(skill_names[:12]) if skill_names else '(see attached CV)'
  1409→
  1410→            summaries = master.get('professional_summaries', {})
  1411→            if isinstance(summaries, dict) and summaries:
  1412→                summary_text = next(iter(summaries.values()))
  1413→            else:
  1414→                summary_text = master.get('summary', '')
  1415→
  1416→            achievements   = master.get('selected_achievements', [])
  1417→            top_ach_titles = '\n'.join(f'- {a.get("title", "")}' for a in achievements[:4]) or '(see CV)'
  1418→
  1419→            answers_snippet = ''
  1420→            answers = conversation.state.get('post_analysis_answers') or {}
  1421→            if answers:
  1422→                answers_snippet = 'Candidate context:\n' + '\n'.join(
  1423→                    f'- {q}: {a}' for q, a in list(answers.items())[:6]
  1424→                )
  1425→
  1426→            tone_hint = _TONE_GUIDANCE.get(tone, '')
  1427→            company   = job_analysis.get('company', 'the company')
  1428→            role      = job_analysis.get('title', 'the position')
  1429→            keywords  = ', '.join((job_analysis.get('ats_keywords') or [])[:12])
  1430→            req_skills = ', '.join((job_analysis.get('required_skills') or [])[:10])
  1431→
  1432→            today = datetime.now().strftime('%B %d, %Y')
  1433→            pieces   = [f'Date: {today}']
  1434→            if company_address:
  1435→                pieces.append(company_address)
  1436→            header_block = '\n'.join(pieces) + '\n\n'  # LLM provides the salutation
  1437→
  1438→            reuse_instruction = (
  1439→                f'\nUse the following prior cover letter as a starting point, '
  1440→                f'adapting it to the new role:\n\n"""\n{reuse_body}\n"""\n'
  1441→            ) if reuse_body else ''
  1442→
  1443→            prompt = f"""\
  1444→You are a professional career coach writing a tailored cover letter.
  1445→
  1446→Tone style: {tone} — {tone_hint}
  1447→
  1448→TARGET ROLE
  1449→  Company: {company}
  1450→  Position: {role}
  1451→  Key requirements: {req_skills or keywords or '(see job description)'}
  1452→
  1453→CANDIDATE PROFILE
  1454→  Name: {personal_info.get('name', 'The candidate')}
  1455→  Summary: {summary_text[:400] if summary_text else '(see CV)'}
  1456→  Top skills: {top_skills}
  1457→  Key achievements:
  1458→{top_ach_titles}
  1459→
  1460→{answers_snippet}
  1461→{reuse_instruction}
  1462→{'Please especially highlight: ' + highlight if highlight else ''}
  1463→
  1464→Write a compelling, personalised cover letter (3–4 paragraphs, ~300–400 words).
  1465→Start directly with the salutation line: "Dear {hiring_manager},"
  1466→Do NOT include a date, address block, or subject line — return only the letter body starting with the salutation.
  1467→Reference concrete skills and achievements from the candidate profile.
  1468→Close professionally with a call to action.
  1469→"""
  1470→
  1471→            try:
  1472→                response = llm_client.chat(
  1473→                    messages=[
  1474→                        {'role': 'system', 'content': 'You write tailored, professional cover letters. Return only the letter body text.'},
  1475→                        {'role': 'user',   'content': prompt},
  1476→                    ],
  1477→                    temperature=0.7,
  1478→                )
  1479→            except Exception as e:
  1480→                return jsonify({'ok': False, 'error': f'LLM error: {e}'}), 500
  1481→
  1482→            # Prepend date + address block; LLM response already contains the salutation
  1483→            letter_text = header_block + response.strip()
  1484→            conversation.state['cover_letter_text']   = letter_text
  1485→            conversation.state['cover_letter_params'] = {
  1486→                'tone': tone, 'hiring_manager': hiring_manager,
  1487→                'company_address': company_address, 'highlight': highlight,
  1488→            }
  1489→        session_registry.touch(sid)
  1490→        return jsonify({'ok': True, 'text': letter_text})
  1491→
  1492→    @app.post("/api/cover-letter/save")
  1493→    def cover_letter_save():
  1494→        """Save cover letter text to DOCX in the output directory and update metadata.json."""
  1495→        entry = _get_session()
  1496→        _validate_owner(entry)
  1497→        conversation = entry.manager
  1498→        sid = entry.session_id
  1499→        with entry.lock:
  1500→            text = (request.get_json(silent=True) or {}).get('text', '').strip()
  1501→            if not text:
  1502→                return jsonify({'error': 'text is required'}), 400
  1503→
  1504→            generated = conversation.state.get('generated_files')
  1505→            if not generated or not generated.get('output_dir'):
  1506→                return jsonify({'error': 'No generated CV found — please generate your CV first.'}), 400
  1507→
  1508→            try:
  1509→                from docx import Document
  1510→                from docx.shared import Pt
  1511→
  1512→                output_dir   = Path(generated['output_dir'])
  1513→                job_analysis = conversation.state.get('job_analysis') or {}
  1514→                company      = (job_analysis.get('company') or 'Company').replace(' ', '_')
  1515→                date_str     = datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
  1516→                filename     = f'CoverLetter_{company}_{date_str}.docx'
  1517→                docx_path    = output_dir / filename
  1518→
  1519→                doc = Document()
  1520→                # Use Normal style and write paragraphs
  1521→                for para_text in text.split('\n'):
  1522→                    p = doc.add_paragraph(para_text)
  1523→                    for run in p.runs:
  1524→                        run.font.size = Pt(11)
  1525→                        run.font.name = 'Calibri'
  1526→                doc.save(str(docx_path))
  1527→
  1528→                # Update metadata.json
  1529→                metadata_path = output_dir / 'metadata.json'
  1530→                if metadata_path.exists():
  1531→                    with open(metadata_path, encoding='utf-8') as f:
  1532→                        metadata = json.load(f)
  1533→                else:
  1534→                    metadata = {}
  1535→                metadata['cover_letter_text']        = text
  1536→                metadata['cover_letter_reused_from'] = conversation.state.get('cover_letter_reused_from')
  1537→                with open(metadata_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
  1538→                    json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)
  1539→
  1540→                conversation.state['cover_letter_text'] = text
  1541→                session_registry.touch(sid)
  1542→                return jsonify({'ok': True, 'filename': filename})
  1543→            except Exception as e:
  1544→                return jsonify({'ok': False, 'error': str(e)}), 500
  1545→
  1546→    # ── Phase 15: Screening Response endpoints ──────────────────────────────
  1547→
  1548→    @app.post("/api/screening/search")
  1549→    def screening_search():
  1550→        """For a single question, find the best prior library match and top 3 relevant experiences."""
  1551→        try:
  1552→            body     = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
  1553→            question = (body.get('question') or '').strip()
  1554→            if not question:
  1555→                return jsonify({'ok': False, 'error': 'question required'}), 400
  1556→
  1557→            from utils.config import get_config
  1558→            config   = get_config()
  1559→            lib_path = Path(config.master_cv_path).parent / 'response_library.json'
  1560→            library: list = []
  1561→            if lib_path.exists():
  1562→                with open(lib_path, encoding='utf-8') as f:
  1563→                    library = json.load(f)
  1564→
  1565→            # Score every library entry against this question
  1566→            scored_prior = sorted(
  1567→                [
  1568→                    {
  1569→                        'score': _text_similarity(
  1570→                            question,
  1571→                            (e.get('question') or '') + ' ' + (e.get('response_text') or ''),
  1572→                        ),
  1573→                        'entry': e,
  1574→                    }
  1575→                    for e in library
  1576→                ],
  1577→                key=lambda x: x['score'],
  1578→                reverse=True,
  1579→            )
  1580→            best_prior = scored_prior[0] if scored_prior and scored_prior[0]['score'] >= 0.25 else None
  1581→
  1582→            # Score experiences from master CV
  1583→            with open(config.master_cv_path, encoding='utf-8') as f:
  1584→                master = json.load(f)
  1585→            exps = master.get('experience', [])
  1586→
  1587→            def _exp_text(e: dict) -> str:
  1588→                return ' '.join([
  1589→                    e.get('title', ''), e.get('company', ''), e.get('summary', ''),
  1590→                    ' '.join(e.get('achievements', [])[:5]),
  1591→                ])
  1592→
  1593→            scored_exps = sorted(
  1594→                [{'score': _text_similarity(question, _exp_text(e)), 'exp': e, 'idx': i}
  1595→                 for i, e in enumerate(exps)],
  1596→                key=lambda x: x['score'],
  1597→                reverse=True,
  1598→            )[:3]
  1599→
  1600→            return jsonify({
  1601→                'ok':     True,
  1602→                'prior':  best_prior['entry'] if best_prior else None,
  1603→                'experiences': [
  1604→                    {
  1605→                        'idx':        x['idx'],
  1606→                        'score':      round(x['score'], 2),
  1607→                        'title':      x['exp'].get('title', ''),
  1608→                        'company':    x['exp'].get('company', ''),
  1609→                        'date_range': x['exp'].get('date_range', ''),
  1610→                        'summary':    (x['exp'].get('summary') or '')[:200],
  1611→                    }
  1612→                    for x in scored_exps
  1613→                ],
  1614→            })
  1615→        except Exception as e:
  1616→            return jsonify({'ok': False, 'error': str(e)}), 500
  1617→
  1618→    @app.post("/api/screening/generate")
  1619→    def screening_generate():
  1620→        """Generate a draft screening-question response via LLM."""
  1621→        try:
  1622→            body            = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
  1623→            question        = (body.get('question') or '').strip()
  1624→            fmt             = body.get('format', 'direct')
  1625→            exp_indices     = body.get('experience_indices') or []
  1626→            prior_response  = (body.get('prior_response') or '').strip()
  1627→
  1628→            if not question:
  1629→                return jsonify({'ok': False, 'error': 'question required'}), 400
  1630→
  1631→            from utils.config import get_config
  1632→            config = get_config()
  1633→            with open(config.master_cv_path, encoding='utf-8') as f:
  1634→                master = json.load(f)
  1635→            exps     = master.get('experience', [])
  1636→            selected = [exps[i] for i in exp_indices if isinstance(i, int) and 0 <= i < len(exps)]
  1637→
  1638→            fmt_name, word_range, fmt_instructions = _SCREENING_FORMAT_GUIDANCE.get(
  1639→                fmt, _SCREENING_FORMAT_GUIDANCE['direct']
  1640→            )
  1641→
  1642→            exp_blocks = '\n\n'.join(
  1643→                f"Role: {e.get('title', '')} at {e.get('company', '')}\n"
  1644→                f"Summary: {(e.get('summary') or '')[:300]}\n"
  1645→                f"Key achievements: {'; '.join((e.get('achievements') or [])[:5])}"
  1646→                for e in selected
  1647→            ) or 'No specific experience provided.'
  1648→
  1649→            entry = _get_session()
  1650→            conversation = entry.manager
  1651→            answers              = conversation.state.get('post_analysis_answers') or {}
  1652→            cover_letter_snippet = (conversation.state.get('cover_letter_text') or '')[:400]
  1653→
  1654→            cl_context = (
  1655→                '\n'.join(f'- {k}: {v}' for k, v in list(answers.items())[:8])
  1656→                if answers else 'None provided.'
  1657→            )
  1658→
  1659→            prior_block = (
  1660→                f'\nUse the following prior response as a starting point, adapting it as needed:\n'
  1661→                f'"""\n{prior_response}\n"""\n'
  1662→            ) if prior_response else ''
  1663→
  1664→            prompt = (
  1665→                f'You are drafting a screening-question response for a job application.\n'
  1666→                f'Response format: {fmt_name} (~{word_range}). {fmt_instructions}\n\n'
  1667→                f'Question:\n"{question}"\n\n'
  1668→                f'Relevant experience:\n{exp_blocks}\n\n'
  1669→                f'Applicant preferences / context:\n{cl_context}\n'
  1670→                + (f'\nCover letter excerpt (for tone/context):\n{cover_letter_snippet}\n' if cover_letter_snippet else '')
  1671→                + prior_block
  1672→                + '\nWrite only the response text. No preamble, labels, or meta-commentary.'
  1673→            )
  1674→
  1675→            try:
  1676→                response_text = llm_client.chat(
  1677→                    messages=[
  1678→                        {'role': 'system', 'content': 'You write concise, tailored screening-question responses for job applications.'},
  1679→                        {'role': 'user',   'content': prompt},
  1680→                    ],
  1681→                    temperature=0.7,
  1682→                )
  1683→            except Exception as e:
  1684→                return jsonify({'ok': False, 'error': f'LLM error: {e}'}), 500
  1685→
  1686→            return jsonify({'ok': True, 'text': response_text.strip()})
  1687→        except Exception as e:
  1688→            return jsonify({'ok': False, 'error': str(e)}), 500
  1689→
  1690→    @app.post("/api/screening/save")
  1691→    def screening_save():
  1692→        """Save screening responses to DOCX, update metadata.json, upsert response_library.json."""
  1693→        entry = _get_session()
  1694→        _validate_owner(entry)
  1695→        conversation = entry.manager
  1696→        orchestrator = entry.orchestrator
  1697→        sid = entry.session_id
  1698→        try:
  1699→            body         = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
```

  1768→    @app.post("/api/copilot-auth/start")
  1769→    def copilot_auth_start():
  1770→        """Begin Device Flow: returns user_code + verification_uri for the user to open."""
  1771→        try:
  1772→            flow = auth_manager.start_device_flow()
  1773→            _auth_poll["device_code"] = flow["device_code"]
  1774→            _auth_poll["interval"]    = flow.get("interval", 5)
  1775→            _auth_poll["error"]       = None
  1776→            return jsonify({
  1777→                "user_code":        flow["user_code"],
  1778→                "verification_uri": flow["verification_uri"],
  1779→                "interval":         flow.get("interval", 5),
  1780→                "expires_in":       flow.get("expires_in", 900),
  1781→            })
  1782→        except Exception as e:
  1783→            return jsonify({"error": str(e)}), 500
  1784→
  1785→    @app.post("/api/copilot-auth/poll")
  1786→    def copilot_auth_poll():
  1787→        """Start a background thread that polls GitHub until the user approves the device flow."""
  1788→        import threading
  1789→        if _auth_poll["polling"]:
  1790→            return jsonify({"ok": True, "message": "Already polling"})
  1791→        device_code = _auth_poll.get("device_code")
  1792→        interval    = _auth_poll.get("interval", 5)
  1793→        if not device_code:
  1794→            return jsonify({"error": "No device flow in progress — call /start first"}), 400
  1795→
  1796→        def _do_poll():
  1797→            _auth_poll["polling"] = True
  1798→            _auth_poll["error"]   = None
  1799→            try:
  1800→                auth_manager.complete_device_flow(device_code, interval)
  1801→            except Exception as exc:
  1802→                _auth_poll["error"] = str(exc)
  1803→            finally:
  1804→                _auth_poll["polling"] = False
  1805→
  1806→        threading.Thread(target=_do_poll, daemon=True).start()
  1807→        return jsonify({"ok": True})
  1808→

  1827→    def get_model():
  1828→        """Return current model, all provider models, and pricing metadata.
  1829→
  1830→        If a `session_id` is supplied (query or JSON body), prefer any
  1831→        session-scoped provider/model persisted in that session's state.

  1835→        session_model = None

  1839→            session_model = conversation.state.get("model")
  1840→
  1841→        provider_for_view = session_provider or _provider_name
  1842→        current = session_model or _current_model or (llm_client.model if hasattr(llm_client, "model") else None)
  1843→        available = _get_available_models(provider_for_view, current_model=current)
  1844→        billing = PROVIDER_BILLING.get(provider_for_view, {"type": "per_token", "note": ""})

  1848→                "model":              m,
  1849→                "context_window":     MODEL_INFO.get(m, {}).get("context_window"),
